1.1: Because many have gone about to compile a narration of the things that have been accomplished among us;
1.2: according as they have delivered unto us, who from the beginning themselves saw and were ministers of the word;
1.3: it seemed good also unto me having diligently attained to all things from the beginning, to write to thee in order, good Theophilus,
1.4: that thou mayst know the verity of those words whereof thou hast been instructed.
1.5: There was in the days of Herod the king of Jewry, a certain Priest named Zacharie, of the course of Abia; & his wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name Elizabeth.
1.6: And they were both just before God, walking in all the commandments and justifications of our Lord without blame,
1.7: and they had no son: for that Elizabeth was barren, and both were well striken in their days.
1.8: And it came to pass, when he executed the priestly function in the order of his course before God,
1.9: according to the custom of the Priestly function, he went forth by lot to offer incense, entering into the temple of our Lord;
1.10: and all the multitude of the people was praying without at the hour of the incense.
1.11: And there appeared to him an Angel of our Lord, standing on the right hand of the Altar of incense.
1.12: And Zacharie was troubled, seeing him; and fear fell upon him.
1.13: But the Angel said to him: Fear not Zacharie, for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elizabeth shall bear thee a son and thou shalt call his name John:
1.14: and thou shalt have joy and exultation, and many shall rejoice in his nativity.
1.15: For he shall be great before our Lord; and wine and sicer he shall not drink; and he shall be replenished with the Holy Ghost even from his mother's womb.
1.16: And he shall convert many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.
1.17: And he shall go before him in the spirit and virtue of Elias, that he may convert the hearts of the fathers unto the children, and the incredulous to the wisdom of the just, to prepare unto the Lord a perfect people.
1.18: And Zacharie said to the Angel: Whereby shall I know this? For I am old; and my wife is well striken in her days.
1.19: And the Angel answering said to him: I am Gabriel that assist before God; and am sent to speak to thee, and to evangelize these things to thee.
1.20: And behold, thou shalt be dumb, and shalt not be able to speak until the day wherein these things shall be done; for-because thou hast not believed my words, which shall be fulfilled in their time.
1.21: And the people was expecting Zacharie; and they marveled that he made tarriance in the temple.
1.22: And coming forth he could not speak to them, and they knew that he had seen a vision in the temple. And he made signs to them, and remained dumb.
1.23: And it came to pass, after the days of his office were expired, he departed into his house.
1.24: And after these days Elizabeth his wife conceived; and hid herself five months, saying:
1.25: For thus hath our Lord done to me in the days wherein he had respect to take away my reproach among men.
1.26: And in the sixth month, the Angel Gabriel was sent of God into a city of Galilee, called Nazareth,
1.27: to a virgin despoused to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin's name was Mary.
1.28: And the Angel being entered in, said unto her: Hail full of grace, our Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.
1.29: Who having heard, was troubled at his saying, and thought what manner of salutation this should be.
1.30: And the Angel said to her: Fear not Mary, for thou hast found grace with God.
1.31: Behold thou shalt conceive in thy womb, and shalt bear a Son; and thou shalt call his name Jesus.
1.32: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the most High, and our Lord God shall give him the seat of David his father:
1.33: and he shall reign in the house of Jacob for ever, and of his Kingdom there shall be no end.
1.34: And Mary said to the Angel: How shall this be done, because I know not man?
1.35: And the Angel answering, said to her: The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee, and the power of the most High shall overshadow thee. And therefore also that which of thee shall be born Holy, shall be called the Son of God.
1.36: And behold Elizabeth thy cousin, she also hath conceived a Son in her old age; and this month, is the sixth to her that is called barren;
1.37: because there shall not be impossible with God any word.
1.38: And Mary said, Behold the handmaid of our Lord, be it done to me according to thy word. And the Angel departed from her.
1.39: And Mary rising up in those days, went unto the hill country with speed into a city of Juda.
1.40: And she entered into the house of Zacharie, and saluted Elizabeth.
1.41: And it came to pass; as Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the infant did leap in her womb. And Elizabeth was replenished with the Holy Ghost:
1.42: and she cried out with a loud voice, and said, Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.
1.43: And whence is this to me, that the mother of my Lord doth come to me?
1.44: For behold as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears, the infant in my womb did leap for joy.
1.45: And Blessed is she that believed because those things shall be accomplished that were spoken to her by our Lord.
1.46: And Mary said: MY Soul doth magnify our Lord:
1.47: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
1.48: Because he hath regarded the humility of his handmaid; for behold from hence forth all generations shall call me blessed.
1.49: Because he that is mighty hath done great things to me: and holy is his name.
1.50: And his mercy from generation unto generations, to them that fear him.
1.51: He hath shewed might in his arm: he hath dispersed the proud in the conceit of their heart.
1.52: He hath deposed the mighty from their seat, & hath exalted the humble.
1.53: The hungry he hath filled with good things: and the rich he hath sent away empty.
1.54: He hath received Israel his child, being mindful of his mercy,
1.55: As he spake to our fathers, to Abraham and his seed for ever.
1.56: And Mary tarried with her about three months: and she returned into her house.
1.57: And Elizabeth's full time was come to be delivered; and she bare a son.
1.58: And her neighbours and kinsfolk heard that our Lord did magnify his mercy with her, and they did congratulate her.
1.59: And it came to pass; on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they called him by his father's name Zacharie.
1.60: And his mother answering, said: Not so, but he shall be called John.
1.61: And they said to her, That there is none in thy kindred that is called by this name.
1.62: And they made signs to his father, what he would have him called.
1.63: And demanding a writing table, he wrote, saying: John is his name. And they all marveled.
1.64: And forthwith his mouth was opened, and his tongue, and he spake blessing God.
1.65: And fear came upon all their neighbours; and all these things were bruited over all the hill-country of Jewry:
1.66: and all that had heard, laid them up in their heart, saying: What an one, trow ye, shall this child be? For the hand of our Lord was with him.
1.67: And Zacharie his father was replenished with the Holy Ghost; and he prophesied, saying:
1.68: Blessed be Our Lord God of Israel: because he hath visited and wrought the redemption of his people:
1.69: And hath erected the horn of salvation to us, in the house of David his servant.
1.70: As he spake by the mouth of his holy Prophets, that are from the beginning:
1.71: Salvation from our enemies, and from the hand of all that hate us:
1.72: To work mercy with our fathers; and to remember his holy testament,
1.73: The oath which he sware to Abraham our father,
1.74: that he would give to us. That without fear being delivered from the hand of our enemies, we may serve him,
1.75: In holiness and justice before him, all our days.
1.76: And thou child, shalt be called the Prophet of the Highest: for thou shalt go before the face of our Lord to prepare his ways.
1.77: To give knowledge of salvation to his people, unto remission of their sins,
1.78: Through the bowels of the mercy of our God, in which the Orient, from on high, hath visited us.
1.79: To illuminate them that sit in darkness, and in the shadow of death: to direct our feet into the way of peace.
1.80: And the child grew, and was strengthened in spirit, and was in the deserts until the day of his manifestation to Israel.

2.1: And it came to pass, in those days there came forth an Edict from Cæsar Augustus that the whole world should be enrolled.
2.2: This first enrolling was made by the President of Syria Cyrinus.
2.3: And all went to be enrolled, every one into his own city.
2.4: And Joseph also went up from Galilee out of the city of Nazareth into Jewry, to the city of David that is called Beth-lehem: for because he was of the house and family of David,
2.5: to be enrolled with Mary his despoused wife that was with child.
2.6: And it came to pass, when they were there, her days were fully come that she should be delivered.
2.7: And she brought forth her first begotten son, and swaddled him in cloths, and laid him down in a manger; because there was not place for them in the inn.
2.8: And there were in the same country shepherds watching, and keeping the night watches over their flock.
2.9: And behold, an Angel of our Lord stood beside them, and the brightness of God did shine round about them, and they feared with a great fear.
2.10: And the Angel said to them: Fear not; for behold I evangelize to you great joy, that shall be to all the people:
2.11: because this day is born to you a Saviour which is Christ our Lord, in the city of David.
2.12: And this shall be a sign to you; You shall find the infant swaddled in cloths, and laid in a manger.
2.13: And suddenly there was with the Angel a multitude of the heavenly army, praising God, and saying,
2.14: Glory in the highest to God: and in earth peace to men of good will.
2.15: And it came to pass, after the Angels departed from them into Heaven, the shepherds spake one to another: Let us go over to Bethlehem, and let us see this word that is done, which our Lord hath shewed to us.
2.16: And they came with speed; and they found Mary and Joseph, and the infant laid in the manger.
2.17: And seeing it, they understood of the word that had been spoken to them concerning this child.
2.18: And all that heard, did marvel; and concerning those things that were reported to them by the shepherds.
2.19: But Mary kept all these words, conferring them in her heart.
2.20: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God in all things that they had heard, and seen, as it was said to them.
2.21: And after eight days were expired, that the child should be circumcised; his name was called Jesus, which was called by the Angel, before that he was conceived in the womb.
2.22: And after the days were fully ended of her purification according to the law of Moyses, they carried him into Jerusalem, to present him to our Lord
2.23: (as it is written in the law of our Lord, That every male opening the matrice, shall be called holy to the Lord. )
2.24: and to give a sacrifice according as it is written in the law of our Lord, a pair of turtles, or two young pigeons.
2.25: And behold there was a man in Jerusalem named Simeon, and this man was just and religious, expecting the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was in him.
2.26: And he had received an answer of the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death unless he saw first the Christ of our Lord.
2.27: And he came in Spirit into the temple. And when his parents brought in the child Jesus, to do according to the custom of the Law for him,
2.28: he also took him into his arms, and blessed God, and said:
2.29: Now Thou doest dismiss thy servant O Lord, according to thy word in peace.
2.30: Because mine eyes have seen thy Salvation,
2.31: Which thou hast prepared before the face of all peoples:
2.32: A light to the revelation of the Gentiles, & the glory of thy people Israel.
2.33: And his father and mother were marveling upon those things which were spoken concerning him.
2.34: And Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary his mother: Behold this is set unto the ruin, and unto the resurrection of many in Israel, & for a sign which shall be contradicted,
2.35: and thine own soul shall a sword pierce, that out of many hearts cogitations may be revealed.
2.36: And there was Anne a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Aser: she was far striken in days, and had lived with her husband seven years from her virginity.
2.37: And she was a widow until eighty and four years; who departed not from the temple, by fastings and prayers serving night and day.
2.38: And she at the same hour suddenly coming in, confessed to our Lord; and spake of him to all that expected the redemption of Israel.
2.39: And after they had wholly done all things according to the law of our Lord, they returned into Galilee, into their city Nazareth.
2.40: And the child grew, and waxed strong: full of wisdom, and the grace of God was in him.
2.41: And his parents went every year unto Jerusalem, at the solemn day of Pasch.
2.42: And when he was twelve years old, they going up into Jerusalem according to the custom of the festival day;
2.43: and having ended the days, when they returned, the child Jesus remained in Jerusalem: and his parents knew it not.
2.44: And thinking that he was in the company, they came a day's journey, and sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance.
2.45: And not finding him, they returned into Jerusalem, seeking him.
2.46: And it came to pass, after three days they found him in the temple sitting in the midst of the Doctors, hearing them and asking them.
2.47: And all were astonished that heard him, upon his wisdom & answers.
2.48: And seeing him, they wondered. And his mother said to him: Son, why hast thou so done to us? Behold thy father and I sorrowing did seek thee.
2.49: And he said to them: What is it that you sought me? Did you not know, that I must be about those things which are my Father's?
2.50: And they understood not the word that he spake unto them.
2.51: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth; and was subject to them. And his mother kept all these words in her heart.
2.52: And Jesus proceeded in wisdom and age, and grace with God and men.

3.1: And in the fifteenth year of the empire of Tiberius Cæsar, Pontius Pilate being Governor of Jewry, and Herod being Tetrarch of Galilee, and Philip his brother Tetrarch of Iturea and the country Trachonitis, and Lysanias Tetrarch of Abilina,
3.2: under the high Priests Annas and Caiphas: the word of our Lord was made upon John the son of Zacharie, in the desert.
3.3: And he came into all the country of Jordan, preaching the baptism of penance unto remission of sins; as it is written in the book of the sayings of Esay the Prophet:
3.4: A voice of one crying in the desert; prepare the way of our Lord, make straight his paths.
3.5: Every valley shall be filled; and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and crooked things shall become straight; and rough ways, plain:
3.6: And all flesh shall see the Salvation of God.
3.7: He said therefore to the multitudes that went forth to be baptized of him: Ye vipers' broods, who hath shewed you to flee from the wrath to come?
3.8: Yield therefore fruits worthy of penance; and do ye not begin to say, we have Abraham, to our father. For I tell you, that God is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.
3.9: And now the axe is put to the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that yieldeth not good fruit, shall be cut down, and cast into fire.
3.10: And the multitudes asked him, saying: What shall we do then?
3.11: And he answering, said unto them: He that hath two coats, let him give to him that hath not; and he that hath meat, let him do likewise.
3.12: And the Publicans also came to be baptized, and said to him: Master, what shall we do?
3.13: But he said to them: Do nothing more than that which is appointed you.
3.14: And the soldiers also asked him, saying: What shall we also do? And he said to them: Vex not, neither calumniate any man; and be content with your stipends.
3.15: And the people imagining, and all men thinking in their hearts of John, lest perhaps he were Christ:
3.16: John answered, saying unto all: I indeed baptize you with water, but there shall come a mightier than I, whose latchet of his shoes I am not worthy to loose; he shall baptize you in the Holy Ghost and fire:
3.17: whose fan is in his hand, and he will purge his floor; and will gather the wheat into his barn, but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.
3.18: Many other things also exhorting did he evangelize to the people.
3.19: And Herod the Tetrarch, when he was rebuked of him for Herodias his brother's wife, and for all the evils which Herod did:
3.20: He added this also above all, and shut up John into prison.
3.21: And it came to pass when all the people was baptized, Jesus also being baptized and praying, Heaven was opened:
3.22: And the Holy Ghost descended in corporal shape as a dove upon him: and a voice from Heaven was made: Thou art my beloved Son, in thee I am well pleased.
3.23: And Jesus himself was beginning to be about thirty years old: as it was thought, the son of Joseph, who was of Heli,
3.24: who was of Matthat, who was of Levi, who was of Melchi, who was of Ianne, who was of Joseph,
3.25: who was of Matthathias, who was of Amos, who was of Naum, who was of Hesli, who was of Nagge,
3.26: who was of Mahath, who was of Matthathias, who was of Semei, who was of Joseph, who was of Juda,
3.27: who was of Johanna, who was of Resa, who was of Zorobabel, who was of Salathiel, who was of Neri,
3.28: who was of Melchi, who was of Addi, who was of Cosam, who was of Elmadan, who was of Her,
3.29: who was of Jesus, who was of Eliezer, who was of Iorim, who was of Matthat, who was of Levi,
3.30: who was of Simeon, who was of Judas, who was of Joseph, who was of Jona, who was of Eliacim.
3.31: who was of Melcha, who was of Menna, who was of Matthatha, who was of Nathan, who was of David,
3.32: who was of Jesse, who was of Obed, who was of Booz, who was of Salmon, who was of Naasson,
3.33: who was of Aminadab, who was of Aram, who was of Esron, who was of Phares, who was of Judas,
3.34: who was of Jacob, who was of Isaac, who was of Abraham, who was of Thare, who was of Nachor,
3.35: who was of Sarug, who was of Ragau, who was of Phaleg, who was of Heber, who was of Sale,
3.36: who was of Cainan, who was of Arphaxad, who was of Sem, who was of Noe, who was of Lamech,
3.37: who was of Mathusale, who was of Henoch, who was of Jared, who was of Malaleel, who was of Cainan,
3.38: who was of Henos, who was of Seth, who was of Adam, who was of God.

4.1: And Jesus full of the Holy Ghost, returned from Jordan, & was driven in the spirit into the desert.
4.2: forty days, and was tempted of the devil. And he did eat nothing in those days; and when they were ended, he was an hungered.
4.3: And the devil said to him: If thou be the Son of God, say to this stone that it be made bread.
4.4: And Jesus made answer unto him: It is written, That not in bread alone shall man live, but in every word of God.
4.5: And the devil brought him into an high mountain, and shewed him all the kingdoms of the whole world in a moment of time;
4.6: and he said to him: To thee will I give this whole power, and the glory of them; for to me they are delivered, and to whom I will, I do give them.
4.7: Thou therefore if thou wilt adore before me, they shall all be thine.
4.8: And Jesus answering said to him: It is written, Thou shalt adore the Lord thy God & him only shalt thou serve.
4.9: And he brought him into Jerusalem, & set him upon the pinnacle of the Temple; and he said to him: If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself from hence downward.
4.10: For it is written, that He hath given his Angels charge of thee, that they preserve thee:
4.11: and that in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest perhaps thou knock thy foot against a stone.
4.12: And Jesus answering said to him: It is said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.
4.13: And all the tentation being ended, the devil departed from him until a time.
4.14: And Jesus returned in the force of the spirit into Galilee, and the fame went forth through the whole country of him.
4.15: And he taught in their Synagogues, and was magnified of all.
4.16: And he came to Nazareth where he was brought up: and he entered according to his custom on the Sabboth day into the Synagogue; and he rose up to read.
4.17: And the book of Esay the Prophet was delivered unto him. And as he unfolded the book, he found the place where it was written:
4.18: The Spirit of the Lord upon me, for which he anointed me, to evangelize unto the poor he sent me, to heal the contrite of heart,
4.19: to preach to the captives remission, and sight to the blind, to dismiss the bruised unto remission, to preach the acceptable year of the Lord, and the day of retribution.
4.20: And when he had folded the book, he rendered it to the minister, and sat down. And the eyes of all in the Synagogue were bent upon him.
4.21: And he began to say unto them: That this day is fulfilled this scripture in your ears.
4.22: And all gave testimony to him; and they marveled in the words of grace that proceeded from his mouth, and they said: Is not this Joseph's son?
4.23: And he said to them: Certes you will say to me this similitude, Physician, cure thyself: as great things as we have heard done in Capharnaum, do also here in thy country.
4.24: And he said: Amen I say to you, that no Prophet is accepted in his own country.
4.25: In truth I say to you, there were many widows in the days of Elias in Israel, when the Heaven was shut three years and six months, when there was a great famine made in the whole earth:
4.26: And to none of them was Elias sent, but into Sarepta of Sidon, to a widow woman.
4.27: And there were many lepers in Israel under Elisæus the Prophet; and none of them was made clean but Naaman the Syrian.
4.28: And all in the Synagogue were filled with anger, hearing these things.
4.29: And they rose, and cast him out of the city: and they brought him to the edge of the hill, where upon their city was built, that they might throw him down headlong.
4.30: But he passing through the midst of them, went his way.
4.31: And he went down into Capharnaum a city of Galilee; and there he taught them on the Sabboths.
4.32: And they were astonished at his doctrine; because his talk was in power.
4.33: And in the Synagogue there was a man having an unclean devil, and he cried out with a loud voice.
4.34: Saying: Let be, what to us and thee Jesus of Nazareth? Art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Saint of God.
4.35: And Jesus rebuked him, saying: Hold thy peace, & go out of him. And when the devil had thrown him into the midst, he went out of him, and hurted him nothing.
4.36: And there came fear upon all, and they talked together one with another, saying: What word is this, that in power and virtue he commandeth the unclean spirits, and they go out?
4.37: And the fame of him was published into every place of the country.
4.38: And Jesus rising up out of the Synagogue, entered into Simon's house. And Simon's wife's mother was holden with a great fever; and they besought him for her.
4.39: And standing over her, he commanded the fever, and it left her. And incontinent rising, she ministered to them.
4.40: And when the sun was down, all that had diseased of sundry maladies, brought them to him. But he imposing hands upon every one, cured them.
4.41: And devils went out from many, crying and saying: That thou art the Son of God. And rebuking them he suffered them not to speak, that they knew he was Christ.
4.42: And when it was day, going forth he went into a desert place; & the multitudes sought him, and came even unto him; and they held him that he should not depart from them.
4.43: To whom he said: That to other cities also must I evangelize the Kingdom of God; because therefore I was sent.
4.44: And he was preaching in the Synagogues of Galilee.

5.1: And it came to pass, when the multitudes pressed upon him to hear the word of God, and himself stood beside the lake of Genesareth.
5.2: And he saw two ships standing by the lake: and the fishers were gone down, and washed their nets.
5.3: And he going up into one ship that was Simon's, desired him to bring it back a little from the land. And sitting, he taught the multitudes out of the ship.
5.4: And as he ceased to speak, he said to Simon: Launch forth into the deep, and let loose your nets to make a draught.
5.5: And Simon answering, said to him: Master, labouring all the night, we have taken nothing; but in thy word I will let loose the net.
5.6: And when they had done this, they inclosed a very great multitude of fishes, and their net was broken.
5.7: And they beckoned to their fellows that were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came and filled both ships, so that they did sink.
5.8: Which when Simon Peter did see, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying: Go forth from me, because I am a sinful man, O Lord.
5.9: For he was wholly astonished and all that were with him, at the draught of fishes which they had taken.
5.10: In like manner also James and John the sons of Zebedee, who were Simon's fellows. And Jesus said to Simon: Fear not; from this time now, thou shalt be taking men.
5.11: And having brought their ships to land, leaving all things they followed him.
5.12: And it came to pass, when he was in one of the cities, & behold a man full of leprosy, and seeing Jesus, and falling on his face, besought him saying: Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
5.13: And stretching forth the hand, he touched him, saying: I will. Be thou made clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
5.14: And he commanded him that he should tell nobody, but, Go, shew thyself to the Priest, and offer for thy cleansing as Moyses commanded, for a testimony to them.
5.15: But the bruit of him went abroad the more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be cured of their infirmities.
5.16: And he retired into the desert, and prayed.
5.17: And it came to pass one day, and he sat teaching. And there were Pharisees sitting and Doctors of Law that were come out of every town of Galilee and Jewry and Jerusalem; and the virtue of our Lord was to heal them.
5.18: And behold men carrying in a bed a man that had the palsy: and they sought to bring him in, and to lay him before him.
5.19: And not finding on which side they might bring him in for the multitude, they went up upon the roof, and through the tiles let him down with the bed into the midst, before Jesus.
5.20: Whose faith when he saw, he said: Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
5.21: And the Scribes and Pharisees began to think, saying: Who is this that speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but only God?
5.22: And when Jesus knew their cogitations, answering he said to them: What do you think in your hearts?
5.23: Which is easier to say, Thy sins are forgiven thee, or to say, Arise, and walk?
5.24: But that you may know that the Son of man hath power in earth to forgive sins (he said to the sick of the palsy) I say to thee, Arise, take up thy bed, and go into thy house.
5.25: And forthwith rising up before them, he took that wherein he lay; and he went into his house, magnifying God.
5.26: And all were astonished: and they magnified God. And they were replenished with fear, saying: That we have seen marvelous things today.
5.27: And after these things he went forth, and saw a Publican called Levi, sitting at the Custom-house, and he said to him: Follow me.
5.28: And leaving all things, he rose and followed him.
5.29: And Levi made him a great feast in his house; and there was a great multitude of Publicans; and of others that were sitting at the table with them.
5.30: And their Pharisees and Scribes murmured, saying to his disciples: Why do you eat and drink with Publicans and sinners?
5.31: And Jesus answering said to them: They that are whole, need not the Physician: but they that are ill at ease.
5.32: I came not to call the just, but sinners to penance.
5.33: But they said to him: Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make obsecrations, and of the Pharisees in like manner; but thine do eat and drink?
5.34: To whom he said: Why, can you make the children of the bridegroom fast whiles the bridegroom is with them?
5.35: But the days will come; and when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, then they shall fast in those days.
5.36: And he said a similitude also unto them: That no man putteth a piece from a new garment into an old garment; otherwise both he breaketh the new, and the piece from the new agreeth not with the old.
5.37: And nobody putteth new wine into old bottles; otherwise the new wine will break the bottles, and itself will be shed, and the bottles will be lost.
5.38: But new wine is to be put into new bottles: and both are preserved together.
5.39: And no man drinking old, will new by and by, for he saith, The old is better.

6.1: And it came to pass on the Sabboth second-first, when he passed through the corn, his Disciples did pluck the ears, & did eat rubbing them with their hands.
6.2: And certain of the Pharisees said to them: Why do you that which is not lawful on the Sabboths?
6.3: And Jesus answering them, said: Neither this have you read which David did, when himself was an hungered & they that were with him:
6.4: how he entered into the house of God, and took the loaves of Proposition, and did eat, and gave to them that were with him, which it is not lawful to eat but only for Priests?
6.5: And he said to them: That the Son of man is Lord of the Sabboth also.
6.6: And it came to pass on another Sabboth also, that he entered into the Synagogue, and taught. And there was a man, and his right hand was withered.
6.7: And the Scribes and Pharisees watched if he would cure on the Sabboth; that they might find how to accuse him.
6.8: But he knew their cogitations; and he said to the man that had the withered hand: Arise, and stand forth into the midst. And rising he stood.
6.9: And Jesus said to them: I ask you, if it be lawful on the Sabboths to do well or ill; to save a soul or to destroy?
6.10: And looking about upon them all, he said to the man: Stretch forth thy hand. And he stretched it forth; & his hand was restored.
6.11: And they were replenished with madness; & they communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.
6.12: And it came to pass in those days, he went forth into the mountain to pray, and he passed the whole night in the prayer of God.
6.13: And when day was come, he called his Disciples; and he chose twelve of them (whom also he named Apostles )
6.14: Simon whom he surnamed Peter, and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philippe and Bartholomew,
6.15: Matthew and Thomas, James of Alphæus and Simon that is called Zelotes,
6.16: and Jude of James, and Judas Iscariote which was the traitor.
6.17: And descending with them he stood in a plain place, and the multitude of his Disciples, and a very great company of people from all Jewry and Jerusalem, and the sea coast both of Tyre & Sidon,
6.18: Which were come to hear him, and to be healed of their maladies. And they that were vexed of unclean spirits, were cured.
6.19: And all the multitude sought to touch him, because virtue went forth from him, and healed all.
6.20: And he lifting up his eyes upon his Disciples, said: Blessed are ye poor: for yours is the Kingdom of God.
6.21: Blessed are you that now are an hungered: because you shall be filled. Blessed are you that now do weep: because you shall laugh.
6.22: Blessed shall you be when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you, and upbraid you, and abandon your name as evil, for the Son of man's sake.
6.23: Be glad in that day and rejoice: for behold, your reward is much in Heaven. For according to these things did their fathers to the Prophets.
6.24: But woe to you that are rich: because you have your consolation.
6.25: Woe to you that are filled: because you shall be hungry. Woe to you that now do laugh: because you shall mourn and weep.
6.26: Woe, when all man shall bless you: For according to these things did their fathers to the false-Prophets.
6.27: But to you I say that do hear: Love your enemies, do good to them that hate you.
6.28: Bless them that curse you, and pray for them that calumniate you.
6.29: And he that striketh thee on the cheek, offer also the other. And from him that taketh away from thee thy robe, prohibit not thy coat also.
6.30: And to every one that asketh thee, give, and of him that taketh away the things that are thine, ask not again.
6.31: And according as you will that men do to you, do you also to them in like manner.
6.32: And if you love them that love you, what thank is to you? For sinners also love those that love them.
6.33: And if ye do good to them that do you good: what thank is to you? For sinners also do this.
6.34: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive; what thank is to you? For sinners also lend unto sinners, for to receive as much.
6.35: But love ye your enemies; do good and lend, hoping for nothing thereby: and your reward shall be much, and you shall be the Sons of the Highest, because himself is beneficial upon the unkind and the evil.
6.36: Be ye therefore merciful as also your Father is merciful.
6.37: Judge not, & you shall not be judged. Condemn not, & you shall not be condemned. Forgive, and you shall be forgiven.
6.38: Give, and there shall be given to you. Good measure & pressed down and shaken together and running over shall they give into your bosom. For with the same measure that you do mete, it shall be measured to you again.
6.39: And he said to them a similitude also: Can the blind lead the blind? Do not both fall into the ditch?
6.40: The Disciple is not above his Master: but every one shall be perfect, if he be as his Master.
6.41: And why seest thou the mote in thy brother's eye: but the beam that is in thine own eye thou considerest not?
6.42: Or how canst thou say to thy brother: Brother, let me cast out the mote out of thine eye: thyself not seeing the beam in thine own eye? Hypocrite, cast first the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to take forth the mote out of thy brother's eye.
6.43: For there is no good tree that yieldeth evil fruits; nor evil tree, that yieldeth good fruit.
6.44: For every tree is known by his fruit. For neither do they gather figs of thorns; neither of a bush do they gather the grape.
6.45: The good man of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth good; and the evil man of the ill treasure bringeth forth evil. For of the aboundance of the heart the mouth speaketh,
6.46: And why call you me, Lord, Lord: and do not the things which I say?
6.47: Every one that cometh to me, and heareth my words, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like.
6.48: He is like to a man building a house, that digged deep, and laid the foundation upon a rock. And when an inundation rose, the river beat against that house, and it could not move it; for it was founded upon a rock.
6.49: But he that heareth, and doeth not; is like to a man building his house upon the earth without a foundation: against the which the river did beat; and incontinent it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.

7.1: And when he had fully said all his words into the ears of the people, he entered into Capharnaum.
7.2: And the servant of a certain Centurion being sick, was ready to die: who was dear unto him.
7.3: And when he had heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the Ancients of the Jews, desiring him to come and heal his servant.
7.4: But they being come to Jesus, besought him earnestly, saying to him, That he is worthy that thou shouldest do this for him.
7.5: For he loveth our Nation; and he hath built a Synagogue for us.
7.6: And Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the Centurion sent his friends unto him, saying: Lord, trouble not thyself. For I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof.
7.7: For the which cause neither did I think myself worthy to come to thee; but say the word, and my servant shall be made whole.
7.8: For I also am a man subject to authority, having under me soldiers: and I say to this, go, and he goeth: and to another, come, and he cometh; and to my servant, do this, and he doeth it.
7.9: Which Jesus hearing, marveled; and turning to the multitudes that followed him he said: Amen I say to you, neither in Israel have I found so great faith.
7.10: And they that were sent, being returned home, found the servant that had been sick, whole.
7.11: And it came to pass, afterward he went into a city that is called Naim; and there went with him his Disciples and a very great multitude.
7.12: And when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold a dead man was carried forth, the only son of his mother; and she was a widow: and a great multitude of the city with her.
7.13: Whom when our Lord had seen, being moved with mercy upon her, he said to her: Weep not.
7.14: And he came near and touched the coffin. And they that carried it stood still; and he said: Young man, I say to thee, arise.
7.15: And he that was dead, sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.
7.16: And fear took them all; and they magnified God, saying, That a great Prophet is risen among us: and, that God hath visited his people.
7.17: And this saying went forth into all Jewry of him, & into all the country about.
7.18: And John's disciples shewed him of all these things.
7.19: And John called two of his disciples, and sent them to Jesus, saying: Art thou he that art to come; or expect we another?
7.20: And when the men were come unto him, they said: John the Baptist hath sent us to thee, saying: Art thou he that art to come; or expect we another?
7.21: (And the self same hour, he cured many of maladies, and hurts, and evil spirits: and to many blind he gave sight.)
7.22: And answering, he said to them: Go & report to John what you have heard and seen: That the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are made clean, the deaf hear, the dead rise again,
7.23: the poor are evangelized: and blessed is he whosoever shall not be scandalized in me.
7.24: And when John's messengers were departed, he began to say of John to the multitudes: What went you out into the desert to see? A reed moved with the wind?
7.25: But what went you forth to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Behold they that are in costly apparel and delicacies, are in the house of kings.
7.26: But what went you out for to see? A Prophet? Certes I say to you, and more than a Prophet.
7.27: This is he of whom it is written: Behold I send mine Angel before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
7.28: For I say to you: A greater Prophet among the children of women than John the Baptist, there is no man. But he that is the lesser in the Kingdom of God, is greater than he.
7.29: And all the people hearing and the Publicans, justified God, being baptized with John's baptism.
7.30: But the Pharisees and the lawyers despised the counsel of God against themselves, being not baptized of him.
7.31: And our Lord said: Where unto then shall I liken the men of this generation, and whereunto are they like?
7.32: They are like to children sitting in the market-place, and speaking one to another, and saying: We have piped to you, and you have not danced; we have lamented, and you have not wept.
7.33: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and you say: He hath a devil.
7.34: The Son of man came eating and drinking; and you say: Behold a man that is a gourmander and a drinker of wine, a friend of Publicans and sinners.
7.35: And wisdom is justified of all her children.
7.36: And one of the Pharisees desired him to eat with him. And being entered into the house of the Pharisee, he sat down to meat.
7.37: And behold a woman that was in the city, a sinner, as she knew that he was set down in the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster box of ointment;
7.38: and standing behind beside his feet, she began to water his feet with tears, & wiped them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, & anointed them with the ointment.
7.39: And the Pharisee that had bid him, seeing it, spake within himself, saying: This man if he were a Prophet, would know certes who and what manner of woman she is which toucheth him, that she is a sinner.
7.40: And Jesus answering said to him: Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. But he said: Master, say.
7.41: Acertain creditor had two debters; one did owe five hundred pence, & the other fifty.
7.42: They having not wherewith to pay, he forgave both. Whether therefore doth love him more?
7.43: Simon answering said: I suppose that he to whom he forgave more. But he said to him: Thou hast judged rightly.
7.44: And turning to the woman, he said unto Simon: Doest thou see this woman? I entered into thy house, water to my feet thou didst not give; but she with tears hath watered my feet, and with her hairs hath wiped them.
7.45: Kiss thou gavest me not; but she since I came in, hath not ceased to kiss my feet.
7.46: With oil thou didst not anoint my head; but she with ointment hath anointed my feet.
7.47: For the which I say to thee: Many sins are forgiven her, because she hath loved much. But to whom less is forgiven, he loveth less.
7.48: And he said to her: Thy sins are forgiven thee.
7.49: And they that sat together at the table began to say within themselves. Who is this that also forgiveth sins?
7.50: And he said to the woman: Thy faith hath made thee safe, go in peace.

8.1: And it came to pass afterward, and he made his journey by cities and towns preaching and evangelizing the Kingdom of God; and the Twelve with him,
8.2: and some women that had been cured of wicked spirits and infirmities; Mary which is called Magdalene, out of whom seven devils were gone forth,
8.3: And Joane the wife of Chusa Herod's procurator, and Susan, and many others that did minister unto him of their substance.
8.4: And when a very great multitude assembled and hastened out of the cities unto him, he said by a similitude.
8.5: The sower went forth to sow his seed. And whiles he soweth, some fell by the way side, and was trodden upon, and the fowls of the air did eat it.
8.6: And other some fell upon the rock; and being shot up, it withered, because it had not moisture.
8.7: And othersome fell among thorns, and the thorns growing up withal, choked it.
8.8: And other some fell upon good ground; and being shot up, yielded fruit an hundred fold. Saying these things he cried: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
8.9: And his Disciples asked him what this parable was.
8.10: To whom he said: To you it is given to know the mystery of the Kingdom of God; but to the rest in parables, that seeing they may not see, and hearing may not understand.
8.11: And the parable is this: The seed, is the word of God.
8.12: And they besides the way, are those that hear, then the devil cometh, and taketh the word out of their heart, lest believing they be saved.
8.13: For they upon the rock; such as when they hear, with joy receive the word: and these have no roots; because for a time they believe, and in time of tentation they revolt.
8.14: And that which fell into thorns, are they that have heard, and going their ways, are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life, and render not fruit.
8.15: And that upon good ground, are they which in a good and very good heart, hearing the word, do retain it, and yield fruit in patience.
8.16: And no man lighting a candle doth cover it with a vessel, or put it under a bed; but setteth it upon a candlestick, that they that enter in, may see the light.
8.17: For there is not any thing secret, that shall not be made manifest; nor hid, that shall not be known, & come abroad.
8.18: See therefore how you hear. For he that hath, to him shall be given; and whosoever hath not, that also which he thinketh he hath, shall be taken away from him.
8.19: And his mother and brethren came unto him; and they could not come at him for the multitude.
8.20: And it was told him: Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desirous to see thee.
8.21: Who answering said to them: My mother and my brethren, are they that hear the word of God and do it.
8.22: And it came to pass one day; and he went up into a boat, and his Disciples, and he said to them: Let us strike over the lake. And they launched forth.
8.23: And when they were sailing, he slept; and there fell a storm of wind into the lake, and they were filled, and were in danger.
8.24: And they came and raised him, saying: Master, we perish. But he rising, rebuked the wind and the tempest of water; and it ceased, and there was made a calm.
8.25: And he said to them: Where is your faith? Who fearing, marveled one to another, saying: Who is this (trow ye) that he commandeth both the winds and the sea, and they obey him?
8.26: And they sailed to the country of the Gerasens which is over against Galilee.
8.27: And when he was come forth to the land, there met him a certain man that had a devil now a very long time, and he did wear no cloths, neither did he tarry in house, but in the monuments.
8.28: And as he saw Jesus, he fell down before him; and crying out with a great voice, he said: What is to me & thee Jesus Son of God most high? I beseech thee do not torment me.
8.29: For he commanded the unclean spirit to go forth out of the man. For many times he caught him, and he was bound with chains, and kept with fetters; and breaking the bonds was driven of the devil into the deserts.
8.30: And Jesus asked him saying: What is thy name? But he said: Legion, because many devils were entered into him.
8.31: And they besought him that he would not command them to go into the depth.
8.32: And there was there a herd of many swine feeding on the mountain; & they desired him that he would permit them to enter into them. And he permitted them.
8.33: The devils therefore went forth out of the man, and entered into the swine; and the herd with violence went headlong into the lake, and was stifled.
8.34: Which when the swineherds saw done, they fled, and told into the city and into the towns.
8.35: And they went forth to see that which was done; and they came to Jesus, and found the man, out of whom the devils were gone forth, sitting at his feet, clothed, and well in his wits, and they were afraid.
8.36: And they also that had seen, told them how he had been made whole from the legion.
8.37: And all the multitude of the country of the Gerasens besought him to depart from them; for they were taken with great fear. And he going up into the boat, returned.
8.38: And the man out of whom the devils were departed, desired him that he might be with him. But Jesus dismissed him, saying:
8.39: Return into thy house, and tell how great things God hath done to thee. And he went through the whole city, preaching how great things Jesus had done to him.
8.40: And it came to pass; when Jesus was returned, the multitude received him. And all were expecting him.
8.41: And behold there came a man whose name was Jairus, and he was Prince of the Synagogue: and he fell at the feet of Jesus, desiring him that he would enter into his house,
8.42: because he had an only daughter almost twelve years old, and she was a dying. And it chanced, whiles he went, he was thronged of the multitudes.
8.43: And there was a certain woman in a flux of blood from twelve years past, which had bestowed all her substance upon Physicians, neither could she be cured of any:
8.44: she came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment; and forthwith the flux of her blood stinted,
8.45: And Jesus said: Who is it that touched me? And all denying, Peter said, and they that were with him: Master, the multitudes throng and press thee, and doest thou say, Who touched me?
8.46: And Jesus said: Somebody hath touched me; for I know that there is virtue proceeded from me.
8.47: And the woman seeing, that she was not hid, came trembling, and fell down before his feet; and for what cause she had touched him, she shewed before all the people, and how forthwith she was made whole.
8.48: But he said to her: Daughter, thy faith hath made thee safe, go thy way in peace.
8.49: As he was speaking, there cometh one to the Prince of the Synagogue, saying to him, That thy daughter is dead, trouble him not.
8.50: And Jesus hearing this word, answered the father of the maid: Fear not; believe only, and she shall be safe.
8.51: And when he was come to the house, he permitted not any man to enter in with him, but Peter, and James, and John, and the father and mother of the maid.
8.52: And all wept, and mourned for her. But he said: Weep not, the maid is not dead, but sleepeth.
8.53: And they derided him, knowing that she was dead.
8.54: But he holding her hand cried saying: Maid arise.
8.55: And her spirit returned, and she rose incontinent. And he bade them give her to eat.
8.56: And her parents were astonished, whom he commanded to tell no man that which was done.

9.1: And calling together the twelve Apostles, he gave them virtue and power over all devils, and to cure maladies.
9.2: And he sent them to preach the Kingdom of God; and to heal the sick.
9.3: And he said to them: Take nothing for the way, neither rod, nor scrip, nor bread, nor money, neither have two coats.
9.4: And into whatsoever house you enter, tarry there, and thence do not depart.
9.5: And whosoever shall not receive you, going forth out of that city, shake off the dust also of your feet for a testimony upon them.
9.6: And going forth they went a circuit from town to town evangelizing and curing every-where.
9.7: And Herod the Tetrarch heard all things that were done by him; and he staggered because it was said of some, That John was risen from the dead.
9.8: But of other some, That Elias hath appeared; and of others, that a Prophet one of the old ones was risen.
9.9: And Herod said: John I have beheaded; but who is this of whom I hear such things? And he sought for to see him.
9.10: And the Apostles being returned, reported to him whatsoever they did: and taking them he retired apart into a desert place, which belongeth to Beth-saida.
9.11: Which the multitudes understanding, followed him & he received them, and spake to them of the Kingdom of God, and them that had need of cure he healed.
9.12: And the day began to draw towards an end. And the Twelve coming near, said to him: Dismiss the multitudes, that going into towns and villages here about, they may have lodging, and find meats; because here we are in a desert place.
9.13: And he said to them: Give you them to eat. But they said: We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; unless perhaps we should go & buy meats for all this multitude.
9.14: And there were men almost five thousand. And he said to his Disciples: Make them sit down by companies fifty and fifty.
9.15: And so they did. And they made all sit down.
9.16: And taking the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up unto Heaven, and blessed them; and he brake, and distributed to his Disciples, for to set before the multitudes.
9.17: And they did all eat, and had their fill. And there was taken up that which remained to them, twelve baskets of fragments.
9.18: And it came to pass; when he was alone praying, his Disciples also were with him; and he asked them saying: Whom do the multitudes say that I am?
9.19: But they answered, and said: John the Baptist; and some, Elias; but some, that one of the Prophets before time, is risen.
9.20: And he said to them: But whom say ye that I am? Simon Peter answering, said: The Christ of God.
9.21: But he rebuking them, commanded that they should tell this to no man,
9.22: saying: That the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the Ancients and chief Priests and Scribes, and be killed, and the third day rise again.
9.23: And he said to all: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me.
9.24: For he that will save his life, shall lose it; for he that shall lose his life for my sake, shall save it.
9.25: For what profit hath a man if he gain the whole world, and lose himself, and cast away himself?
9.26: For he that shall be ashamed of me and of my words, him the Son of man shall be ashamed of, when he shall come in his majesty, and his Father's, and of the holy Angels.
9.27: And I say to you assuredly: There be some standing here that shall not taste death, till they see the Kingdom of God.
9.28: And it came to pass after these words almost eight days, and he took Peter and James and John, and went into a mountain to pray.
9.29: And whiles he prayed, the shape of his countenance was altered: and his raiment white and glistering.
9.30: And behold two men talked with him. And they were Moyses and Elias,
9.31: appearing in majesty. And they told his decease that he should accomplish in Jerusalem.
9.32: But Peter and they that were with him, were heavy with sleep. And awaking, they saw his majesty, and the two men that stood with him.
9.33: And it came to pass, when they departed from him, Peter said to Jesus: Master, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moyses, and one for Elias: not knowing what he said.
9.34: And as he spake these things, there came a cloud, and overshadowed them: and they feared, when they entered into the cloud.
9.35: And a voice was made out of the cloud, saying: This is my beloved Son, hear him.
9.36: And whiles the voice was made, Jesus was found alone. And they held their peace, and told no man in those days any of these things which they had seen.
9.37: And it came to pass the day following, when they came down from the mountain, there met him a great multitude.
9.38: And behold a man of the multitude cried out, saying: Master, I beseech thee, look upon my son because he is mine only one.
9.39: And lo, the spirit taketh him, and he suddenly cryeth, and he dasheth him, and teareth him that he foameth, and with much ado departeth renting him.
9.40: And I desired thy Disciples to cast him out, and they could not.
9.41: And Jesus answering said: O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and suffer you? Bring hither thy son.
9.42: And when he came to him, the devil dashed, and tore him. And Jesus rebuked the unclean Spirit, and healed the lad, and rendered him to his father.
9.43: And all were astonished at the might of God; and all marveling at all things that he did, he said to his Disciples:
9.44: Lay you in your hearts these words, for it shall come to pass that the Son of man shall be delivered into the hands of men.
9.45: But they did not know this word, and it was covered before them, that they perceived it not. And they were afraid to ask him of this word.
9.46: And there entered a cogitation into them, which of them should be greater.
9.47: But Jesus seeing the cogitations of their heart, took a child and set him by him,
9.48: and said to them, whosoever receiveth this child in my name, receiveth me, and whosoever receiveth me, receiveth him that sent me. For he that is the lesser among you all, he is the greater.
9.49: And John answering said: Master, we saw a certain man casting out devils in thy name, and we prohibited him, because he followeth not with us.
9.50: And Jesus said to him: Prohibit not. For he that is not against you, is for you.
9.51: And it came to pass, whiles the days of his assumption were accomplishing, and he fixed his face to go into Jerusalem.
9.52: And he sent messengers before his face; and going they entered into a city of the Samaritans to prepare for him.
9.53: And they received him not, because his face was to go to Jerusalem.
9.54: And when his Disciples James and John had seen it, they said: Lord wilt thou we say that fire come down from Heaven and consume them?
9.55: And turning, he rebuked them, saying: You know not of what spirit you are.
9.56: The Son of man came not to destroy souls, but to save. And they went into another town.
9.57: And it came to pass as they walked in the way, a certain man said to him: I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
9.58: Jesus said to him: The foxes have holes, and the fowls of the air nests; but the Son of man hath not where to repose his head.
9.59: But he said to another: Follow me. And he said: Lord, permit me first to go, and to bury my father.
9.60: And Jesus said to him: Let the dead bury their dead; but go thou, set forth the Kingdom of God.
9.61: And another said: I will follow thee Lord, but permit me first to take my leave of them that are at home.
9.62: Jesus said to him: No man putting his hand to the plough, and looking back, is apt for the Kingdom of God.

10.1: And after this our Lord designed also other seventy two: and he sent them two and two before his face into every city and place whither himself would come.
10.2: And he said to them: The harvest truly is much; but the workmen few. Desire therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send workmen into his harvest.
10.3: Go: Behold I send you as lambs among wolves.
10.4: Carry not purse nor scrip, nor shoes; and salute nobody by the way.
10.5: Into whatsoever house you enter, first say: Peace to this house.
10.6: And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon him: but if not, it shall return to you.
10.7: And in the same house tarry you, eating and drinking such things as they have. For the workman is worthy of his hire. Remove not from house to house.
10.8: And into what city soever you enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you;
10.9: and cure the sick that are in it, and say to them: The Kingdom of God is come nigh upon you.
10.10: And into whatsoever city you enter, and they receive you not, going forth into the streets thereof, say:
10.11: The dust also of your city that cleaveth to us, we do wipe off against you. Yet this know ye that the Kingdom of God is at hand.
10.12: Isay to you, it shall be more tolerable for Sodom in that day, than for that city.
10.13: Woe to thee Corozaim, woe to thee Beth-saida: for if in Tyre and Sidon had been wrought the miracles that have been wrought in you, they had done penance sitting in sack cloth and ashes long ago.
10.14: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgement, than for you.
10.15: And thou Capharnaum that art exalted unto Heaven: thou shalt be thrust down even unto hell.
10.16: He that heareth you, heareth me; and he that despiseth you, despiseth me. And he that despiseth me, despiseth him that sent me.
10.17: And the Seventy-two returned with joy, saying: Lord, the devils also are subject to us in thy name.
10.18: And he said to them: I saw Satan as a lightning fall from Heaven.
10.19: Behold, I have given you power to tread upon serpents, and scorpions, and upon all the power of the enemy, and nothing shall hurt you.
10.20: But yet rejoice not in this, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rejoice in this, that your names are written in Heaven.
10.21: In that very hour he rejoiced in spirit, and said: I confess to thee O Father, Lord of Heaven and earth, because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them to little ones. Yea Father, for so hath it well pleased thee.
10.22: All things are delivered to me of my Father. And no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and to whom the Son will reveal.
10.23: And turning to his Disciples, he said: Blessed are the eyes that see the things that you see.
10.24: For I say to you, that many Prophets and kings desired to see the things that you see, and saw them not; and to hear the things that you hear, and heard them not.
10.25: And behold a certain lawyer stood up, tempting him and saying: Master, by doing of what thing shall I possess life everlasting?
10.26: But he said to him: In the law what is written? How readest thou?
10.27: He answering said: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart, and with thy whole soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind: and thy neighbour as thyself.
10.28: And he said to him: Thou hast answered right, this do and thou shalt live.
10.29: But he desirous to justify himself, said to Jesus: And who is my neighbour?
10.30: And Jesus taking it, said: A certain man went down from Jerusalem into Jericho, and fell among thieves, who also spoiled him, and giving him wounds went away leaving him half-dead.
10.31: And it chanced that a certain Priest went down the same way; and seeing him, passed by.
10.32: In like manner also a Levite, when he was near the place, and saw him, passed by.
10.33: But a certain Samaritane going his journey, came near him; and seeing him, was moved with mercy.
10.34: And going unto him, bound his wounds, pouring in oil and wine: and setting him upon his own beast, brought him into an inn, and took care of him.
10.35: And the next day he took forth two pence, and gave to the host, and said: Have care of him; and whatsoever thou shalt supererogate, I at my return will repay thee.
10.36: Which of these three in thy opinion was neighbour to him that fell among thieves?
10.37: But he said: He that did mercy upon him. And Jesus said to him: Go, and do thou in like manner.
10.38: And it came to pass as they went, and he entered into a certain town; and a certain woman named Martha, received him into her house,
10.39: and she had a sister called Mary. Who sitting also at our Lord's feet, heard his word.
10.40: But Martha was busy about much service. Who stood and said: Lord, hast thou no care that my sister hath left me alone to serve? Speak to her therefore, that she help me.
10.41: And our Lord answering said to her: Martha, Martha, thou art careful, and art troubled about many things.
10.42: But one thing is necessary, Mary hath chosen the best part which shall not be taken away from her.

11.1: And it came to pass, when he was in a certain place praying, as he ceased, one of his Disciples said to him: Lord teach us to pray, as John also taught his Disciples.
11.2: And he said to them: When you pray, say, Father, sanctified be thy name. Thy Kingdom come,
11.3: Our daily bread give us this day,
11.4: and forgive us our sins, for because ourselves also do forgive every one that is in debt to us. And lead us not into temptation.
11.5: And he said to them: Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go to him at midnight, and shall say to him, Friend, lend me three loaves,
11.6: because a friend of mine is come out of his way to me, and I have not what to set before him:
11.7: and he from within answering saith: Trouble me not, now the door is shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.
11.8: And if he shall persevere knocking, I say to you, although he will not rise and give him because he is his friend, yet for his importunity he will rise, and give him as many as he needeth.
11.9: And I say to you, Ask, and it shall be given you: seek, and you shall find: knock, and it shall be opened to you.
11.10: For every one that asketh, receiveth: and he that seeketh, findeth: and to him that knocketh, it shall be opened.
11.11: And which of you if he ask his father bread, will he give him a stone? Or a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?
11.12: Or if he ask an egg, will he reach him a scorpion?
11.13: If you then being naught, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father from Heaven give the good spirit to them that ask him?
11.14: And he was casting out a devil, and that was dumb. And when he had cast out the devil, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marveled.
11.15: And certain of them said: In Beel-zebub the prince of devils he casteth out devils.
11.16: And other tempting, asked of him a sign from Heaven.
11.17: But he seeing their cogitations, said to them: Every kingdom divided against itself, shall be made desolate, and house upon house, shall fall.
11.18: And if Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? Because you say that in Beel-zebub I do cast out devils.
11.19: And if I in Beel-zebub cast out devils: your children, in whom do they cast out? Therefore they shall be your judges.
11.20: But if I in the finger of God do cast out devils; surely the Kingdom of God is come upon you.
11.21: When the strong armed keepeth his court, those things are in peace that he possesseth.
11.22: But if a stronger than he come upon him and overcome him; he will take away his whole armour wherein he trusted, and will distribute his spoils.
11.23: He that is not with me, is against me: and he that gathereth not with me, scattereth.
11.24: When the unclean spirit shall depart out of a man, he wandereth through places without water, seeking rest. And not finding, he saith, I will return into my house whence I departed.
11.25: And when he is come, he findeth it swept with a besom, and trimmed.
11.26: Then he goeth and taketh seven other spirits worse than himself, and entering in they dwell there. And the last of that man be made worse than the first.
11.27: And it came to pass, when he said these things, a certain woman lifting up her voice out of the multitude said to him: Blessed is the womb that bare thee, and the paps that thou didst suck.
11.28: But he said: Yea rather, blessed are they that hear the word of God, and keep it.
11.29: And the multitudes running together, he began to say: This generation, is a wicked generation: it asketh a sign, and a sign shall not be given it but the sign of Jonas the Prophet.
11.30: For as Jonas was a sign to the Ninivites; so shall the Son of man also be to this generation.
11.31: The queen of the South shall rise in the judgement with the men of this generation, and shall condemn them: because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Salomon. And behold, more than Salomon here.
11.32: The men of Ninivee shall rise in the judgement with this generation, and shall condemn it, because they did penance at the preaching of Jonas. And behold, more than Jonas here.
11.33: No man lighteth a candle, and putteth it in secret, neither under a bushel: but upon a candlestick, that they that go in may see the light.
11.34: The candle of thy body is thine eye. If thine eye be simple, thy whole body shall be lightsome: but if it be naught, thy body also shall be darksome.
11.35: See therefore that the light which is in thee, be not darkness.
11.36: If then thy whole body be lightsome, having no part of darkness: it shall be lightsome wholly, and as a bright candle it shall lighten thee.
11.37: And when he was speaking, a certain Pharisee desired him that he would dine with him. And he going in sat down to eat.
11.38: And the Pharisee began to think within himself and to say: Why he was not washed before dinner.
11.39: And our Lord said to him: Now you Pharisees do make clean that on the out side of the cup and of the platter; but that of yours which is within, is full of rapine and iniquity.
11.40: Fools, did not he that made that on the out side, make that also that is on the inside?
11.41: But yet that that remaineth, give alms, & behold all things are clean unto you.
11.42: But woe to you Pharisees, because you tithe mint and rew and every herb: and pass over judgement and the charity of God. But these things you ought to have done, and not to omit those.
11.43: Woe to you Pharisees, because you love the first chairs in the Synagogues, and salutations in the market place.
11.44: Woe to you, because you are as monuments that appear not, and men walking over, are not ware.
11.45: And one of the Lawyers answering saith to him: Master, in saying these things, thou speakest to our reproach also.
11.46: But he said: Woe to you Lawyers also: because you load men with burdens which they cannot bear, and yourselves touch not the packs with one of your fingers.
11.47: Woe to you that build the monuments of the Prophets: and your fathers did kill them.
11.48: Surely you do testify that you consent to the works of your fathers: because they indeed did kill them, and you build their sepulchres.
11.49: For this cause the wisdom also of God said, I will send to them Prophets and Apostles, and of them they will kill and persecute.
11.50: That the blood of all the Prophets that was shed from the making of the world, may be required of this generation,
11.51: from the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharie that was slain between the altar and the temple. Yea I say to you, it shall be required of this generation.
11.52: Woe to you Lawyers, because you have taken away the key of knowledge: yourselves have not entered, & those that did enter you have prohibited.
11.53: And when he said these things to them, the Pharisees & the Lawyers began vehemently to urge him, & to stop his mouth about many things,
11.54: lying in wait for him, and seeking to catch something of his mouth, that they might accuse him.

12.1: And when great multitudes stood about him, so that they trod one another, he began to say to his Disciples: Take good heed of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
12.2: For nothing is hid, that shall not be revealed: nor secret, that shall not be known.
12.3: For the things that you have said in darkness, shall be said in the light: and that which you have spoken into the ear in the chambers, shall be preached in the house-tops.
12.4: And I say to you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after this have no more to do.
12.5: But I will shew you whom ye shall fear: fear him who after he hath killed, hath power to cast into hell. Yea I say to you, fear him.
12.6: Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and one of them is not forgotten before God?
12.7: Yea the hairs also of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: you are more worth than many sparrows.
12.8: And I say to you, Every one that confesseth me before men, the Son of man also will confess him before the Angels of God.
12.9: But he that denieth me before men, shall be denied before the Angels of God.
12.10: And every one that speaketh a word against the Son of man it shall be forgiven him: but he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost, to him it shall not be forgiven.
12.11: And when they shall bring you into the Synagogues and to Magistrates and Potestates, be not careful in what manner, or what you shall answer, or what you shall say.
12.12: For the Holy Ghost shall teach you in the very hour what you must say.
12.13: And one of the multitude said to him: Master, speak to my brother that he divide the inheritance with me.
12.14: But he said to him: Man, who hath appointed me judge or divider over you?
12.15: And he said to them: See and beware of all avarice: for not in any man's aboundance doth his life consist, of those things which he possesseth.
12.16: And he spake a similitude to them, saying: A certain rich man's field yielded plenty of fruits.
12.17: And he thought within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have not whither to gather my fruits?
12.18: And he said: This will I do, I will destroy my barns, and will make greater, and thither will I gather all things that are grown to me, and my goods.
12.19: And I will say to my soul; Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years, take thy rest, eat, drink, make good cheer.
12.20: But God said to him, Thou fool, this night they require thy soul of thee; and the things that thou hast provided, whose shall they be?
12.21: So is he that layeth up treasure to himself, and is not rich to God-ward.
12.22: And he said to his Disciples: Therefore I say to you, Be not careful for your life, what you shall eat; nor for your body, what you shall do on.
12.23: The life is more than the meat, and the body is more than the raiment.
12.24: Consider the ravens, for they sow not, neither do they reap, which neither have storehouse nor barn, and God feedeth them. How much more are you of greater price than they?
12.25: And which of you by caring can add to his stature one cubit?
12.26: If then you be not able to do so much as the least thing, for the rest why are you careful?
12.27: Consider the lilies how they grow: they labour not, neither do they spin. But I say to you, Neither Salomon in all his glory was arrayed as one of these.
12.28: And if the grass that today is in the field, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, God so clotheth; how much more you, O ye of little faith?
12.29: And you, do not seek what you shall eat, or what you shall drink: and be not lifted up on high.
12.30: For all these things the Nations of the world do seek. But your Father knoweth that you have need of these things.
12.31: But seek first the Kingdom of God, and all these things shall be given you besides.
12.32: Fear not little flock, for it hath pleased your Father to give you a Kingdom.
12.33: Sell the things that you possess, and give alms. Make to you purses that wear not, treasure that wasteth not, in Heaven: whither the thief approacheth not, neither doth the moth corrupt.
12.34: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
12.35: Let your loins be girded, and candles burning in your hands,
12.36: and you like to men expecting their Lord, when he shall return from the marriage: that when he doth come and knock, forthwith they may open unto him.
12.37: Blessed are those servants, whom when the Lord cometh, he shall find watching. Amen I say to you, that he will gird himself, and make them sit down, and passing will minister unto them.
12.38: And if he come in the second watch, and if in the third watch he come, and so find, blessed are those servants.
12.39: And this know ye, that if the householder did know what hour the thief would come, he would watch verily, and would not suffer his house to be broken up.
12.40: Be you also ready: for at what hour you think not, the Son of man will come.
12.41: And Peter said to him: Lord, doest thou speak this parable to us, or likewise to all?
12.42: And our Lord said: Who (thinkest thou) is a faithful steward and wise, whom the Lord appointeth over his family, to give them in season their measure of wheat?
12.43: Blessed is that servant, whom when the Lord cometh, he shall find so doing.
12.44: Verily I say to you, that over all things which he possesseth, he shall appoint him.
12.45: But if that servant say in his heart, My Lord is long a coming; and shall begin to strike the servants and handmaids, and eat and drink, and be drunk:
12.46: the Lord of that servant shall come in a day that he hopeth not, and at an hour that he knoweth not, and shall divide him, and shall appoint his portion with the infidels.
12.47: And that servant that knew the will of his Lord, and prepared not himself, & did not according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.
12.48: But he that knew not, and did things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few. And every one to whom much was given, much shall be required of him: and to whom they committed much, more will they demand of him.
12.49: I came to cast fire on the earth; & what will I, but that it be kindled?
12.50: But I have to be baptized with a baptism: and how am I straitened until it be dispatched?
12.51: Think you that I came to give peace on the earth? No, I tell you, but separation.
12.52: For there shall be from this time, five in one house divided: three against two, and two against three.
12.53: There shall be divided, the father against the son, and the son against his father, the mother against the daughter, and the daughter against the mother, the mother in law against her daughter in law, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
12.54: And he said also to the multitudes, when you see a cloud rising from the west, by and by you say, A shower cometh, and so it cometh to pass:
12.55: and when the south wind blowing, you say, That there will be heat: & it cometh to pass.
12.56: Hypocrites, the face of the Heaven and of the earth you have skill to discern: but this time how do you not discern?
12.57: And why of yourselves also judge you not that which is just?
12.58: And when thou goest with thy adversary to the Prince, in the way endeavour to be delivered from him: lest perhaps he draw thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the exactor, and the exactor cast thee into prison.
12.59: Isay to thee, thou shalt not go out thence, until thou pay the very last mite.

13.1: And there were certain present at that very time telling him of the Galilæans, whose blood Pilate mingled with their sacrifices.
13.2: And he answering said to them: Think you that these Galilæans were sinners more than all the Galilæans, that they suffered such things?
13.3: No, I say to you: but unless you have penance, you shall all likewise perish.
13.4: As those eighteen upon whom the tower fell in Siloe, and slew them: think you that they also were debters above all the men that dwell in Jerusalem?
13.5: No, I say to you: but if you have not penance you shall all likewise perish.
13.6: And he said this similitude: A certain man had a figtree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking for fruit on it, and found not.
13.7: And he said to the dresser of the vineyard, Lo it is three years since I come seeking for fruit upon this figtree, and I find not. Cut it down therefore; whereto doth it also occupy the ground?
13.8: But he answering said to him: Lord, let it alone this year also, until I dig about it, and dung it.
13.9: And if happily it yield fruit: but if not, hereafter thou shalt cut it down.
13.10: And he was teaching in their Synagogue on the Sabboths.
13.11: And behold a woman that had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years: and she was crooked, neither could she look upward at all.
13.12: Whom when Jesus saw, he called her unto him, and said to her: Woman, thou art delivered from thy infirmity.
13.13: And he imposed hands upon her, and forthwith she was made straight and glorified God.
13.14: And the Archsynagogue answering (because he had indignation that Jesus had cured on the Sabboth) said to the multitude: Six days there are wherein you ought to work. In them therefore come, and be cured; and not in the Sabboth day.
13.15: And our Lord answering to him, said: Hypocrite, doth not every one of you upon the Sabboth loose his ox or his ass from the manger, and leadeth them to water?
13.16: But this daughter of Abraham whom Satan hath bound, lo, these eighteen years, ought not she to be loosed from this bond on the Sabboth day?
13.17: And when he said these things, all his adversaries were ashamed: and all the people rejoiced in all things that were gloriously done of him.
13.18: He said therefore: Whereunto is the Kingdom of God like, and whereunto shall I esteem it like?
13.19: It is like to a mustard seed, which a man took and cast into his garden and it grew, and became a great tree, & the fowls of the air rested in the boughs thereof.
13.20: And again he said: Like to what shall I esteem the Kingdom of God?
13.21: It is like to leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
13.22: And he went by the cities and towns teaching, and making his journey unto Jerusalem.
13.23: And a certain man said to him: Lord, be they few that are saved? But he said to them:
13.24: Strive to enter by the narrow gate: because many, I say to you, shall seek to enter, and shall not be able.
