13.25: But when the good-man of the house shall enter in, and shut the door, and you shall begin to stand without, and knock at the door, saying, Lord open to us: and he answering shall say to you, I know you not whence you are:
13.26: then you shall begin to say: We did eat before thee and drink, and in our streets didst thou teach.
13.27: And he shall say to you, I know you not whence you are, depart from me all ye workers of iniquity.
13.28: There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth: when you shall see Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, and all the Prophets in the Kingdom of God, and you to be thrust out.
13.29: And there shall come from the East and West & the North and the South; and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
13.30: And behold, they are last that shall be first, and they be first that shall be last.
13.31: The same day there came certain of the Pharisees, saying to him: Depart and get thee hence, because Herod will kill thee.
13.32: And he said to them: Go, and tell that fox, Behold I cast out devils, and perfit cures this day and tomorrow, and the third day I am consummate.
13.33: But yet I must walk this day and tomorrow and the day following, because it cannot be that a Prophet perish out of Jerusalem.
13.34: Jerusalem, Jerusalem which killest the Prophets, and stonest them that are sent to thee, how often would I gather thy children as the bird doth her brood under her wings, and thou wouldest not?
13.35: Behold your house shall be left desert to you. And I say to you, that you shall not see me till it come when you shall say: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of our Lord.

14.1: And it came to pass when Jesus entered into the house of a certain Prince of the Pharisees upon the Sabboth to eat bread, and they watched him.
14.2: And behold there was a certain man before him that had the dropsy.
14.3: And Jesus answering, spake to the Lawyers and Pharisees, saying: Is it Lawful to cure on the Sabboth?
14.4: But they held their peace. But he taking him, healed him, and sent him away.
14.5: And answering them he said: Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit; and will not incontinent draw him out on the Sabboth day?
14.6: And they could not answer him to these things.
14.7: And he spake to them also that were invited a parable, marking how they chose the first seats at the table, saying to them:
14.8: When thou art invited to a marriage, sit not down in the first place, lest perhaps a more honourable than thou be invited of him;
14.9: and he that bade thee and him, come and say to thee, Give this man place: and then thou begin with shame to take the last place.
14.10: But when thou art bidden, go, sit down in the lowest place: that when he that invited thee cometh, he may say to thee, Friend sit up higher: then shalt thou have glory before them that sit at table with thee.
14.11: because every one that exalteth himself, shall be humbled: and he that humbleth himself, shall be exalted.
14.12: And he said to him also that had invited him: When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, nor kinsmen, nor thy neighbours that are rich: lest perhaps they also invite thee again, and recompence be made to thee.
14.13: But when thou makest a feast, call the poor, feeble, lame, and blind,
14.14: and thou shalt be blessed, because they have not to recompence thee: for recompence shall be made thee in the resurrection of the just.
14.15: When one of them that sat at the table with him, had heard these things, he said to him: Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the Kingdom of God.
14.16: But he said to him: A certain man made a great supper, and called many.
14.17: And he sent his servant at the hour of supper to say to the invited, that they should come, because now all things are ready.
14.18: And they began all at once to make excuse. The first said to him, I have bought a farm, and I must needs go forth and see it; I pray thee hold me excused.
14.19: And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them; I pray thee, hold me excused.
14.20: And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.
14.21: And the servant returning told these things to his Lord. Then the Master of the house being angry, said to his servant: Go forth quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and the poor and feeble and blind and lame bring in hither.
14.22: And the servant said: Lord, it is done as thou didst command, and yet there is place.
14.23: And the Lord said to the servant: Go forth into the ways and hedges; and compel them to enter, that my house may be filled.
14.24: But I say to you, that none of those men that were called, shall taste my supper.
14.25: And great multitudes went with him; and turning, he said to them:
14.26: If any man come to me and hateth not his father and mother, and wife and children, and brethren and sisters, yea and his own life besides; he cannot be my disciple.
14.27: And he that doth not bear his cross and come after me; cannot be my Disciple.
14.28: For, which of you minding to build a tower, doth not first sit down and reckon the charges that are necessary, whether he have to finish it:
14.29: lest, after that he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish it, all that see it, begin to mock him,
14.30: saying, That this man began to build, and he could not finish it?
14.31: Or what king about to go to make war against another king, doth not first sit down and think whether he be able with ten thousands to meet him that with twenty thousands cometh against him?
14.32: Otherwise whiles he is yet far off, sending a legacy he asketh those things that belong to peace.
14.33: So therefore every one of you that doth not renounce all that he possesseth, cannot be my disciple.
14.34: Salt is good. But if the salt leese his virtue, wherewith shall it be seasoned?
14.35: It is profitable neither for the ground, nor for the dunghill, but it shall be cast forth. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

15.1: And there approached Publicans and sinners unto him for to hear him.
15.2: And the Pharisees and the Scribes murmured saying: That this man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
15.3: And he spake to them this parable, saying:
15.4: What man of you having an hundred sheep: and if he hath lost one of them, doth he not leave the ninety nine in the desert, and goeth after that which was lost until he find it?
15.5: And when he hath found it, layeth it upon his shoulders rejoicing:
15.6: and coming home calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying to them: Rejoice with me, because I have found my sheep that was lost?
15.7: Isay to you, that even so there shall be joy in Heaven upon one sinner that doth penance, than upon ninety nine just that need not penance.
15.8: Or what woman having ten groats: if she leese one groat, doth she not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently, until she find?
15.9: And when she hath found, calleth together her friends and neighbours, saying: Rejoice with me, because I have found the groat which I had lost?
15.10: So I say to you, there shall be joy before the Angels of God upon one sinner that doth penance.
15.11: And he said: A certain man had two sons;
15.12: and the younger of them said to his father: Father, give me the portion of substance that belongeth to me. And he divided unto them the substance.
15.13: And not many days after the younger son gathering all his things together went from home into a far country: and there he wasted his substance, living riotously.
15.14: And after he had spent all, there fell a sore famine in that country, and he began to be in need.
15.15: And he went, and cleaved to one of the citizens of that country. And he sent him into his farm to feed swine.
15.16: And he would fain have filled his belly of the husks that the swine did eat; and nobody gave unto him.
15.17: And returning to himself he said: How many of my father's hirelings have aboundance of bread, and I here perish for famine?
15.18: I will arise, and will go to my father, and say to him: Father, I have sinned against Heaven and before thee:
15.19: I am not now worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hirelings.
15.20: And rising up he came to his father. And when he was yet far off, his father saw him, and was moved with mercy, and running to him fell upon his neck, and kissed him.
15.21: And his son said to him: Father, I have sinned against Heaven & before thee, I am not now worthy to be called thy son.
15.22: And the father said to his servants: Quickly bring forth the first stole, and do it on him, and put a ring upon his hand, and shoes upon his feet:
15.23: and bring the fatted calf, and kill it, and let us eat, and make merry:
15.24: because this my son was dead, and is revived: was lost, and is found. And they began to make merry.
15.25: But his elder son was in the field, and when he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard music and dancing:
15.26: and he called one of the servants, and asked what these things should be.
15.27: And he said to him: Thy brother is come, and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe.
15.28: But he had indignation, and would not go in. His father therefore going forth began to desire him.
15.29: But he answering said to his father: Behold, so many years do I serve thee, and I never transgressed thy commandment, and thou didst never give me a kid to make merry with my friends:
15.30: but after that this thy son, that hath devoured his substance with whores, is come, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.
15.31: But he said to him: Son, thou art always with me, and all my things are thine.
15.32: But it behoved us to make merry and be glad because this thy brother was dead, and is revived, was lost, and is found.

16.1: And he said also to his Disciples: There was a certain rich man that had a bailiff: & he was ill reported of unto him, as he that had wasted his goods.
16.2: And he called him, and said to him: What hear I this of thee? Render account of thy bailiffship: for now thou canst no more be bailiff.
16.3: And the bailiff said within himself: What shall I do, because my lord taketh away from me the bailiffship? Dig I am not able, to beg I am ashamed.
16.4: I know what I will do, that when I shall be removed from the bailiffship, they may receive me into their houses.
16.5: Therefore calling together every one of his lord's debters, he said to the first: How much doest thou owe my lord?
16.6: But he saith: An hundred pipes of oil. And he said to him: Take thy bill and sit down, quickly write fifty.
16.7: After that he said to another: But thou, how much doest thou owe? Who said: An hundreth quarters of wheat. He said to him: Take thy bill, and write eighty.
16.8: And the lord praised the bailiff of iniquity, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world, are wiser than the children of light in their generation.
16.9: And I say to you: Make unto you friends of the mammon of iniquity that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tabernacles.
16.10: He that is faithful in the least, is faithful in the greater also: and he that is unjust in little, is unjust in the greater also.
16.11: If then you have not been faithful in the unjust mammon, with that which is the true who may credit you?
16.12: And if you have not been faithful in other men's, that which is yours, who will give you?
16.13: No servant can serve two masters; for either he shall hate the one, and love the other; or cleave to one, and contemn the other. You cannot serve God and mammon.
16.14: And the Pharisees which were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.
16.15: And he said to them: You are they that justify yourselves before men, but God knoweth your hearts, because that which is high to men, is abomination before God.
16.16: The Law and the Prophets, unto John. From that time the Kingdom of God is evangelized, and every one doth force toward it.
16.17: And it is easier for Heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fall.
16.18: Every one that dismisseth his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and he that marrieth her that is dismissed from her husband, committeth adultery.
16.19: There was a certain rich man, & he was clothed with purple and silk: and he fared every day magnifically.
16.20: And there was a certain beggar called Lazarus, that lay at his gate, full of sores:
16.21: desiring to be filled of the crumbs, that fell from the rich man's table, but the dogs also came, and licked his sores.
16.22: And it came to pass that the beggar died, and was carried of the Angels into Abraham's bosom. And the rich man also died: and he was buried in hell.
16.23: And lifting up his eyes, when he was in torments, he saw Abraham a far off, and Lazarus in his bosom:
16.24: and he crying said: Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus that he may dip the tip of his finger into water for to cool my tongue, because I am tormented in this flame.
16.25: And Abraham said to him: Son, remember that thou didst receive good things in thy life time, and Lazarus likewise evil: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.
16.26: And beside all these things, between us and you there is fixed a great chaos: that they which will pass from hence to you, may not, neither go from thence hither.
16.27: And he said: Then, father, I beseech thee that thou wouldest send him unto my father's house, for I have five brethren,
16.28: for to testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torments.
16.29: And Abraham said to him: They have Moyses and the Prophets: let them hear them.
16.30: But he said: No, father Abraham, but if some man shall go from the dead to them, they will do penance.
16.31: And he said to him: If they hear not Moyses and the Prophets, neither if one shall rise again from the dead, will they believe.

17.1: And he said to his Disciples: It is impossible that scandals should not come: but woe to him by whom they come.
17.2: It is more profitable for him, if a mill-stone be put about his neck, and he be cast into the sea, than that he scandalize one of these little ones.
17.3: Look well to yourselves. If thy brother sin against thee, rebuke him: and if he do penance, forgive him.
17.4: And if he sin against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day be converted unto thee, saying, It repenteth me, forgive him.
17.5: And the Apostles said to our Lord: Increase faith in us.
17.6: And our Lord said: If you had faith like to a mustard-seed, you might say to this mulberry tree, be thou rooted up, and be transplanted into the sea: and it would obey you.
17.7: And which of you having a servant plowing or keeping cattle, that will say to him returning out of the field: Pass quickly, sit down:
17.8: and saith not to him: Make ready supper, and gird thyself, and serve me whiles I eat and drink, and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?
17.9: Doth he give that servant thanks, for doing the things which he commanded him?
17.10: Itrow not: So you also, when you shall have done all things that are commanded you, say: We are unprofitable servants; we have done that which we ought to do.
17.11: And it came to pass, as he went unto Jerusalem, he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
17.12: And when he entered into a certain town, there met him ten men that were lepers, who stood a far off;
17.13: and they lifted up their voice, saying: Jesus Master, have mercy on us.
17.14: Whom as he saw, he said: Go, shew yourselves to the Priests. And it came to pass, as they went, they were made clean.
17.15: And one of them as he saw that he was made clean, went back with a loud voice magnifying God,
17.16: and he fell on his face before his feet, giving thanks: and this was a Samaritane.
17.17: And Jesus answering said: Were not ten made clean? and where are the nine?
17.18: There was not found that returned, and gave glory to God, but this stranger.
17.19: And he said to him: Arise, go thy ways; because thy faith hath made thee safe.
17.20: And being asked of the Pharisees: when cometh the Kingdom of God? he answered them and said: The Kingdom of God cometh not with observation:
17.21: neither shall they say: Lo here, or lo there. For lo the Kingdom of God is within you.
17.22: And he said to his Disciples: The days will come when you shall desire to see one day of the Son of man; and you shall not see.
17.23: And they will say to you: Lo here and lo there. Go not, neither do ye follow after.
17.24: For even as the lightning that lighteneth from under Heaven, unto those parts that are under Heaven, shineth: so shall the Son of man be in his day.
17.25: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected of this generation.
17.26: And as it came to pass in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.
17.27: They did eat and drink, they did marry wives and were given to marriage even until the day that Noe entered into the ark: and the flood came, and destroyed them all.
17.28: Likewise as it came to pass in the days of Lot: They did eat and drink, bought and sold, planted, and builded:
17.29: and in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire & brimstone from Heaven, & destroyed them all:
17.30: according to these things it shall be in the day that the Son of man shall be revealed.
17.31: In that hour he that shall be in the house-top, and his vessels in the house, let him not go down to take them up: & he that is in the field, in like manner let him not return back.
17.33: Whosoever seeketh to save his life, shall lose it: and Whosoever doth lose the same, shall quicken it.
17.34: Isay to you, in that night there shall be two in one bed: the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left:
17.35: two women shall be grinding together: the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left. two in the field: the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.
17.36: They answering say to him: Where Lord?
17.37: Who said to them: Wheresoever the body shall be, thither will the eagles also be gathered together.

18.1: And he spake also a parable to them that it behoveth always to pray, & not to be weary,
18.2: saying: There was a certain judge in a certain city, which feared not God, and of man made no account.
18.3: And there was a certain widow in that city, and she came to him, saying: Revenge me of mine adversary.
18.4: And he would not of a long time. But afterward he said within himself: Although I fear not God, nor make account of man,
18.5: yet because this widow is importune upon me, I will revenge her, lest at the last she come and defame me.
18.6: And our Lord said: Hear what the judge of iniquity sayeth.
18.7: And will not God revenge his elect that cry to him day and night: and will he have patience in them?
18.8: Isay to you that he will quickly revenge them. But yet the Son of man coming, shall he find trow you, faith in the earth?
18.9: And he said also to certain that trusted in themselves as just, and despised others, this parable:
18.10: Two men went up into the Temple to pray: the one a Pharisee, and the other a Publican.
18.11: The Pharisee standing, prayed thus with himself: God, I give thee thanks that I am not as the rest of men, extortioners, unjust, adulterers, as also this Publican.
18.12: I fast twice in a week: I give tithes of all that I possess.
18.13: And the Publican standing a far off would not so much as lift up his eyes toward Heaven; but he knocked his breast, saying: God be merciful to me a sinner.
18.14: Isay to you, this man went down into his house justified more than he: because every one that exalteth himself, shall be humbled; and he that humbleth himself, shall be exalted.
18.15: And they brought unto him infants also, that he might touch them. Which thing when the Disciples saw, they rebuked them.
18.16: But Jesus calling them together, said: Suffer children to come unto me, and forbid them not, for the Kingdom of Heaven is for such.
18.17: Amen I say to you: Whosoever receiveth not the Kingdom of God as a child, shall not enter into it.
18.18: And a certain Prince asked him, saying: Good Master, by doing what, shall I possess everlasting life?
18.19: And Jesus said to him: Why doest thou call me good? None is good but only God.
18.20: Thou knowest the commandments: Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness, Honour thy father & mother.
18.21: Who said: All these things have I kept from my youth.
18.22: Which Jesus hearing, said to him: Yet one thing thou lackest: Sell all that ever thou hast, & give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in Heaven: and come follow me.
18.23: He hearing these things, was stroken sad: because he was very rich.
18.24: And Jesus seeing him stroken sad, said: How hardly shall they that have money enter into the Kingdom of God?
18.25: For it is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
18.26: And they that heard, said: And who can be saved?
18.27: He said to them: The things that are impossible with men, are possible with God.
18.28: And Peter said: Lo, we have left all things, and have followed thee.
18.29: Who said to them: Amen I say to you, there is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children for the Kingdom of God,
18.30: and shall not receive much more in this time, and in the world to come life everlasting.
18.31: And Jesus took the Twelve, and said to them: Behold we go up to Jerusalem, and all things shall be consummate which were written by the Prophets of the Son of man.
18.32: For he shall be delivered to the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and scourged, and spit upon:
18.33: and after they have scourged him, they will kill him, and the third day he shall rise again.
18.34: And they understood none of these things, and this word was hid from them, and they understood not the things that were said.
18.35: And it came to pass, when he drew nigh to Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way, begging.
18.36: And when he heard the multitude passing by, he asked what this should be.
18.37: And they told him that Jesus of Nazareth passed by.
18.38: And he cried saying: Jesus son of David, have mercy upon me.
18.39: And they that went before, rebuked him, that he should hold his peace. But he cried much more, Son of David have mercy upon me.
18.40: And Jesus standing commanded him to be brought unto him. And when he was come near, he asked him,
18.41: saying: What wilt thou that I do to thee? but he said: Lord, that I may see.
18.42: And Jesus said to him: Do thou see; thy faith hath made thee whole.
18.43: And forthwith he saw, and followed him, magnifying God. And all the people as they saw it, gave praise to God.

19.1: And entering in, he walked through Jericho.
19.2: And behold a man named Zachæus: and this was a Prince of the Publicans, and he rich.
19.3: And he sought to see Jesus what he was, and he could not for the multitude, because he was little of stature.
19.4: And running before, he went up into a sycamore tree that he might see him: because he was to pass by it.
19.5: And when he was come to the place, Jesus looking up, saw him, and said to him: Zachæus come down in haste: because this day I must abide in thy house.
19.6: And he in haste came down, and received him rejoicing.
19.7: And when all saw it, they murmured saying, that he turned in, to a man that was a sinner.
19.8: But Zachæus standing, said to our Lord: Behold the half of my goods, Lord, I give to the poor: and if I have defrauded any man of any thing, I restore fourfold.
19.9: Jesus said to him: That this day salvation is made to this house: because that he also is the son of Abraham.
19.10: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
19.11: They hearing these things, he added and spake a parable, for that he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that forthwith the Kingdom of God should be manifested.
19.12: He said therefore: A certain noble man went into a far country to take to himself a kingdom, and to return.
19.13: And calling his ten servants, he gave them ten pounds, and said to them: Occupy till I come.
19.14: And his citizens hated him: and they sent a legacy after him, saying: We will not have this man reign over us.
19.15: And it came to pass after he returned, having received his kingdom: and he commanded his servants to be called, to whom he gave the money; that he might know how much every man had gained by occupying.
19.16: And the first came, saying: Lord thy pound hath gotten ten pounds.
19.17: And he said to him: Well fare thee good servant, because thou hast been faithful in a little, thou shalt have power over ten cities.
19.18: And the second came saying: Lord, thy pound hath made five pounds.
19.19: And he said to him: And be thou over five cities.
19.20: And another came, saying: Lord, lo here thy pound, which I have had laid up in a napkin.
19.21: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou didst not set down, and thou reapest that which thou didst not sow.
19.22: He saith to him: By thine own mouth I judge thee, naughty servant. Thou didst know that I am an austere man, taking up that I set not down, and reaping that which I sowed not:
19.23: and why didst thou not give my money to the bank, and I coming might certes with usury have exacted it?
19.24: And he said to them that stood by: Take the pound away from him, and give it to him that hath ten pounds
19.25: And they said to him: Lord, he hath ten pounds.
19.26: But I say to you, that to every one that hath shall be given: and from him that hath not, that also which he hath shall be taken from him.
19.27: But as for those mine enemies that would not have me reign over them, bring them hither; and kill them before me.
19.28: And having said these things, he went before ascending to Jerusalem.
19.29: And it came to pass when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethania unto the mount called Olivet, he sent two of his Disciples,
19.30: saying: Go into the town which is over against, into the which as you enter, you shall find the colt of an ass tied, on which no man ever hath sitten: loose him, and bring him.
19.31: And if any man ask you: Why loose you him? You shall say thus to him: Because our Lord needeth his service.
19.32: And they that were sent, went their ways, and found as he said to them, the colt standing.
19.33: And when they loosed the colt, the owners thereof said to them: Why loose you the colt?
19.34: But they said: Because our Lord hath need of him.
19.35: And they brought him to Jesus. And casting their garments upon the colt, they set Jesus thereupon.
19.36: And as he went, they spread their garments underneath in the way.
19.37: And when he approached now to the descent of mount-Olivet, all the multitudes of them that descended, began with joy to praise God with a loud voice, for all the miracles that they had seen,
19.38: saying: Blessed is he that cometh King in the name of our Lord, peace in Heaven, and glory on high.
19.39: And certain Pharisees of the multitudes said to him: Master, rebuke thy disciples.
19.40: To whom he said: I say to you, that if these hold their peace, the stones shall cry.
19.41: And as he drew near, seeing the city, he wept upon it, saying,
19.42: Because if thou also hadst known, and that in this thy day, the things that pertain to thy peace: but now they are hid from thine eyes.
19.43: For the days shall come upon thee: and thy enemies shall compass thee with a trench, and inclose thee about, and straiten thee on every side,
19.44: and beat thee flat to the ground, and thy children that are in thee: and they shall not leave in thee a stone upon a stone, because thou hast not known the time of thy visitation.
19.45: And entering into the Temple, he began to cast out the sellers therein and the buyers,
19.46: saying to them: It is written, That my house is the house of prayer. But you have made it a den of thieves.
19.47: And he was teaching daily in the Temple. And the chief Priests and the Scribes and the Princes of the people sought to destroy him:
19.48: and they found not what to do to him. For all the people was suspense, hearing him.

20.1: And it came to pass in one of the days, when he was teaching the people in the Temple & evangelizing, the chief Priests & the Scribes with the Ancients assembled,
20.2: and spake saying to him, Tell us, in what power doest thou these things? Or, who is he that hath given thee this power?
20.3: And Jesus answering, said to them: I also will ask you one word. Answer me,
20.4: The baptism of John was it from Heaven, or of men?
20.5: But they thought within themselves, saying: That if we say, From Heaven, he will say: Why then did you not believe him?
20.6: But if we say, of men, the whole people will stone us: for they are certain that John is a Prophet.
20.7: And they answered that they knew not whence it was.
20.8: And Jesus said to them: Neither do I tell you in what power I do these things.
20.9: And he began to say to the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, & let it out to husbandmen: and he was from home a long time.
20.10: And in time he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard. Who beating him sent him away empty.
20.11: And again he sent another servant. But they beating him also and reproachfully abusing him, sent him away empty.
20.12: And again he sent the third: who wounding him also, cast him out.
20.13: And the Lord of the vineyard said: What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: perhaps when they shall see him, they will reverence him.
20.14: Whom when the husbandmen saw, they thought within themselves, saying: This is the heir, let us kill him, that the heritage may be ours.
20.15: And casting him forth out of the vineyard, they killed him. What therefore will the Lord of the vineyard do to them?
20.16: He will come, and will destroy these husbandmen, and will give the vineyard to others. Which they hearing, said to him: God forbid.
20.17: But he beholding them said: What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become into the head of the corner?
20.18: Every one that falleth upon this stone, shall be quashed: and upon whom it shall fall, it shall break him to powder.
20.19: And the chief Priests and Scribes sought to lay hands upon him that hour: and they feared the people, for they knew that he spake this similitude to them.
20.20: And watching, they sent spies which should feign themselves just: that they might take him in his talk, and deliver him to the principality and power of the President.
20.21: And they asked him, saying: Master, we know that thou speakest and teachest rightly; and thou doest not accept person, but teachest the way of God in truth.
20.22: Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Cæsar, or not?
20.23: But considering their guile, he said to them: Why tempt you me?
20.24: Shew me a penny. Whose image hath it and inscription? They answering said: Cæsar's.
20.25: And he said to them: Render therefore the things that are Cæsar's, to Cæsar: and the things that are God's, to God.
20.26: And they could not reprehend his word before the people: and marveling at his answer, they held their peace.
20.27: And there came certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is a resurrection, and they asked him,
20.28: saying: Master, Moyses gave us in writing: If a man's brother die having a wife, and he have no children, that his brother take her to wife, and raise up seed to his brother.
20.29: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.
20.30: And the next took her, & he died without child.
20.31: And the third took her. In like manner also all the seven, and they left no seed, and died.
20.32: Last of all the woman died also.
20.33: In the resurrection therefore, whose wife shall she be of them? Sithens the seven had her to wife.
20.34: And Jesus said to them: The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:
20.35: But they that shall be counted worthy of that world and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor take wives;
20.36: neither can they die any more, for they are equal to Angels: and they are the sons of God, seeing they are the sons of the resurrection.
20.37: But that the dead rise again, Moyses also shewed, beside the bush, as he calleth the Lord: The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
20.38: For God is not of the dead, but of the living: for all live to him.
20.39: And certain of the Scribes answering, said to him: Master, thou hast said well.
20.40: And further they durst not ask him anything.
20.41: But he said to them: How say they that Christ is the son of David,
20.42: and David himself saith in the book of Psalms: The Lord said to my Lord, sit on my right hand,
20.43: till I put thine enemies, the footstool of thy feet?
20.44: David then calleth him Lord: and how is he his son?
20.45: And all the people hearing him, he said to his Disciples:
20.46: Beware of the Scribes, that will walk in robes, and love salutations in the market-place, and the first chairs in the synagogues, and the chief rooms in feasts.
20.47: Which devour widows' houses: feigning long prayer. These shall receive greater damnation.

21.1: And beholding, he saw them that did cast their gifts into the treasury, rich persons.
21.2: And he saw also a certain poor widow casting two brass mites.
21.3: And he said: Verily I say to you, that this poor widow hath cast more than all.
21.4: For all these of their aboundance have cast into the gifts of God: but she of her penury, hath cast in all her living that she had.
21.5: And certain saying of the temple that it was adorned with goodly stones and donaries, he said:
21.6: These things which you see, the days will come wherein there shall not be left a stone upon a stone that shall not be destroyed.
21.7: And they asked him, saying: Master, when shall these things be: and what shall be the sign when they shall begin to come to pass?
21.8: Who said: See you be not seduced. For many will come in my name, saying that I am he: and the time is at hand, go not therefore after them.
21.9: And when you shall hear of wars and seditions, be not terrified: these things must first come to pass, but the end is not yet by and by.
21.10: Then he said to them: Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
21.11: And there shall be great earth-quakes in places, and pestilences and famines, and terrors from Heaven, and there shall be great signs.
21.12: But before all these things they will lay their hands upon you: and persecute you delivering you into Synagogues and prisons, drawing you to kings and Presidents for my name.
21.13: and it shall happen unto you for testimony.
21.14: Lay up this therefore in your hearts, not to premeditate how you shall answer.
21.15: For I will give you mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to resist and gainsay.
21.16: And you shall be delivered up of your parents and brethren, and kinsmen and friends: and they will put to death of you.
21.17: And you shall be odious to all men for my name:
21.18: and a hair of your head shall not perish.
21.19: In your patience you shall possess your souls.
21.20: And when you shall see Jerusalem compassed about with an army, then know that the desolation thereof is at hand.
21.21: then they that are in Jewry, let them flee to the mountains: and they in the midst thereof, let them depart: and they in the countries, let them not enter into it.
21.22: For these are the days of vengeance, that all things may be fulfilled that are written.
21.23: But woe to them that are with child & that give suck in those days. For there shall be great affliction upon the land, and wrath on this people.
21.24: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword; and shall be led captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden of the Gentiles, till the times of nations be fulfilled.
21.25: And there shall be signs in the sun and the moon and the stars: and upon earth distress of nations, for the confusion of the sound of sea and waves,
21.26: men withering for fear and expectation, what shall come upon the whole world. For the powers of Heaven shall be moved:
21.27: and then they shall see the Son of man coming in a cloud with great power and majesty.
21.28: But when these things begin to come to pass, look up and lift up your heads: because your redemption is at hand.
21.29: And he spake to them a similitude. See the figtree and all trees:
21.30: when they now bud forth fruit out of themselves, you know that summer is nigh.
21.31: So you also when you shall see these things come to pass, know that the Kingdom of God is nigh.
21.32: Amen I say to you, that this generation shall not pass, till all be done.
21.33: Heaven and earth shall pass; but my words shall not pass.
21.34: And look well to yourselves, lest perhaps your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting and drunkenness and cares of this life: and that day come upon you suddenly.
21.35: For as a snare shall it come upon all that sit upon the face of all the earth.
21.36: Watch therefore, praying at all times, that you may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that are to come, and to stand before the Son of man.
21.37: And the days he was teaching in the temple: but the nights going forth, he abode in the mount that is called Olivet.
21.38: And all the people in the morning went unto him in the temple to hear him.

22.1: And the festival day of the Azymes approached, which is called Pasch:
22.2: & the chief Priests & the Scribes sought how they might kill him: but they feared the people.
22.3: And Satan entered into Judas that was surnamed Iscariote, one of the Twelve.
22.4: And he went, and talked with the chief Priests and the Magistrates, how he might betray him to them.
22.5: And they were glad, and bargained to give him money.
22.6: And he promised. And he sought opportunity to betray him apart from the multitudes.
22.7: And the day of the Azymes came, wherein it was necessary that the Pasch should be killed.
22.8: And he sent Peter and John, saying: Go and prepare us the Pasch, that we may eat.
22.9: But they said: Where wilt thou that we prepare it?
22.10: And he said to them: Behold, as you enter into the city, there shall meet you a man carrying a pitcher of water: follow him into the house into which he entereth,
22.11: and you shall say to the good-man of the house: The Master saith to thee, where is the inn where I may eat the Pasch with my Disciples?
22.12: And he will shew you a great refectory adorned: and there prepare.
22.13: And they going, found as he said to them, and prepared the Pasch.
22.14: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve Apostles with him.
22.15: And he said to them: With desire I have desired to eat this Pasch with you before I suffer.
22.16: For I say to you, that from this time I will not eat it, till it be fulfilled in the Kingdom of God.
22.17: And taking the chalice he gave thanks, and said: Take and divide among you.
22.18: For I say to you, that I will not drink of the generation of the vine, till the Kingdom of God do come.
22.19: And taking bread, he gave thanks, and brake; and gave to them, saying: This is my Body Which is Given For You. Do this for a commemoration of me.
22.20: In like manner the chalice also, after he had supped, saying: This is The Chalice The New Testament in my Blood, Which Shall be Shed For You.
22.21: But yet behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me, is with me on the table.
22.22: And the Son of man indeed goeth according to that which is determined: but yet woe to that man by whom he shall be betrayed.
22.23: And they began to question among themselves, which of them it should be that should do this.
22.24: And there fell also a contention between them, which of them seemed to be greater.
22.25: And he said to them: The Kings of the Gentiles overrule them; and they that have power upon them, are called beneficial.
22.26: But you not so: but he that is the greater among you, let him become as the younger: & he that is the leader, as the waiter.
22.27: For which is greater, he that sitteth at the table, or he that ministereth? Is not he that sitteth? But I am in the midst of you, as he that ministereth:
22.28: & you are they that have remained with me in my tentations.
22.29: And I dispose to you, as my Father disposed to me, a Kingdom:
22.30: that you may eat & drink upon my table in my Kingdom, & may sit upon thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
22.31: And our Lord said: Simon, Simon, behold Satan hath required to have you for to sift as wheat:
22.32: But I have Prayed For Thee, that thy faith fail not: and thou once converted, confirm thy brethren.
22.33: Who said to him: Lord, with thee I am ready to go both into prison and unto death.
22.34: And he said: I say to thee Peter, the cock shall not crow today, till thou deny thrice that thou knowest me.
22.35: And he said to them: When I sent you without purse and scrip and shoes, did you lack any thing? But they said: Nothing.
22.36: He said therefore unto them: But now he that hath a purse, let him take it, likewise also a scrip: and he that hath not, let him sell his coat, and buy a sword.
22.37: For I say to you, that yet this that is written must be fulfilled in me: And with the wicked was he reputed. For those things that are concerning me, have an end.
22.38: But they said: Lord, lo two swords here. But he said to them: It is enough.
22.39: And going forth he went according to his custom into mount-Olivet. And his Disciples also followed him.
22.40: And when he was come to the place, he said to them: Pray, lest ye enter into tentation.
22.41: And he was pulled away from them a stone's cast: and kneeling he prayed,
22.42: saying: Father, if thou wilt, transfer this chalice from me. But yet not my will, but thine be done.
22.43: And there appeared to him an Angel from Heaven, strengthening him. And being in an agony, he prayed the longer.
22.44: And his sweat became as drops of blood trickling down upon the earth.
22.45: And when he was risen up from prayer, and was come to his Disciples, he found them sleeping for pensiveness.
22.46: And he said to them: Why sleep you? Arise, pray, lest you enter into tentation.
22.47: As he was yet speaking, behold a multitude: and he that was called Judas, one of the Twelve, went before them, and approached to Jesus, for to kiss him.
22.48: And Jesus said to him: Judas with a kiss doest thou betray the Son of man?
22.49: And they that were about him, seeing what would be, said to him: Lord, shall we strike with the sword?
22.50: And one of them smote the servant of the high Priest: and cut off his right ear.
22.51: But Jesus answering, said: Suffer ye thus far. And when he had touched his ear, he healed him.
22.52: And Jesus said to them that were come unto him, the chief Priests, and magistrates of the temple, & ancients: As it were to a thief are you come forth with swords and clubs?
22.53: When I was daily with you in the temple, you did not lay hands upon me, but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
22.54: And apprehending him, they led him to the high Priest's house: but Peter followed a far off.
22.55: And a fire being kindled in the midst of the court, & they sitting about it, Peter was in the midst of them.
22.56: Whom when a certain wench saw sitting at the light, and had beheld him, she said: This fellow also was with him.
22.57: But he denied him, saying: Woman, I know him not.
22.58: And after a while another man seeing him, said: And thou art of them. But Peter said: O man I am not.
22.59: And after the space as it were of one hour, a certain other man affirmed, saying: Verily this fellow also was with him: for he is also a Galilæan.
22.60: And Peter said: Man I know not what thou sayest. And incontinent as he was yet speaking, the cock crew.
22.61: And our Lord turning looked on Peter. And Peter remembered the word of our Lord, as he had said: That before the cock crow, thou shalt thrice deny me.
22.62: And Peter going forth a doors, wept bitterly.
22.63: And the men that held him, mocked him, beating him.
22.64: And they did blind-fold him, and smote his face. And they asked him saying: Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
22.65: And blaspheming many other things they said against him.
22.66: And when it was day, there assembled the Ancients of the people and chief Priests and Scribes, and they brought him into their Council, saying:
22.67: If thou be Christ tell us. And he said to them: If I tell you, you will not believe me:
22.68: if also I ask, you will not answer me, nor dismiss me.
22.69: But from henceforth the Son of man shall be sitting on the right hand of the power of God.
22.70: And they all said: Art thou then the Son of God? Who said: You say that I am.
22.71: But they said: What need we testimony any further? For ourselves have heard of his own mouth.

23.1: And all the multitude of them rising up, led him to Pilate.
23.2: And they began to accuse him, saying: We have found this man subverting our nation, & prohibiting to give tributes to Cæsar, and saying that he is Christ the King.
23.3: And Pilate asked him, saying: Art thou the King of the Jews? But he answering said: Thou sayest.
23.4: And Pilate said to the chief Priests and multitudes: I find no cause in this man.
23.5: But they were more earnest, saying: He stirreth the people teaching throughout all Jewry, beginning from Galilee even hither.
23.6: But Pilate hearing Galilee, asked if the man were of Galilee.
23.7: And when he understood that he was of Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him back to Herod, who was also himself at Jerusalem in those days.
23.8: And Herod seeing Jesus, was very glad, for he was desirous of a long time to see him, for because he heard many things of him: and he hoped to see some sign wrought by him.
23.9: And he asked him in many words. But he answered him nothing.
23.10: And there stood the chief Priests and the Scribes constantly accusing him.
23.11: And Herod with his army set him at naught: and he mocked him, putting on him a white garment, and sent him back to Pilate.
23.12: And Herod and Pilate were made friends that day. For before they were enemies one to another.
23.13: And Pilate calling together the chief Priests and Magistrates, and the people,
23.14: said to them: You have presented unto me this man, as averting the people, and behold I examining him before you, have found no cause in this man of those things, wherein you accuse him.
23.15: No, nor Herod neither. For I sent you to him, and behold, nothing worthy of death is done to him.
23.16: I will chasten him therefore and dismiss him.
23.17: And he of necessity had to release unto them upon the feast day, one.
23.18: But the whole multitude together cried out, saying: Dispatch him, and release us Barabbas.
23.19: Who was for a certain sedition made in the city and murder, cast into prison.
23.20: And Pilate again spake to them, desirous to release Jesus.
23.21: But they cried again, saying: Crucify, crucify him.
23.22: And he the third time said to them: Why, what evil hath this man done? I find no cause of death in him. I will correct him therefore & let him go.
23.23: But they were instant with loud voices requiring that he might be crucified. And their voices prevailed.
23.24: And Pilate adjudged their petition to be done.
23.25: And he released unto them him that for murder and sedition had been cast into prison, whom they demanded: but Jesus he delivered to their pleasure.
23.26: And when they led him, they took one Simon of Cyrene coming from the country: and they laid the Cross upon him to carry after Jesus.
23.27: And there followed him a great multitude of people, and of women which bewailed and lamented him.
23.28: But Jesus turning to them, said: Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not upon me, but weep upon yourselves, and upon your children.
23.29: For behold the days shall come, wherein they will say: Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that have not born, & the paps that have not given suck.
23.30: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall upon us: and to the hills, Cover us.
23.31: For if in the green wood they do these things, in the dry what shall be done?
23.32: And there were led also other two malefactors with him, to be executed.
23.33: And after they came to the place which is called Calvary, there they crucified him: and the thieves, one on the right hand and the other on the left.
23.34: And Jesus said: Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do. But they dividing his garments, did cast lots.
23.35: And the people stood beholding, and the Princes with them derided him, saying: Others he hath saved, let him save himself, if this be Christ, the elect of God.
23.36: And the soldiers also mocked him coming to him, and offering him vinegar,
23.37: saying: If thou be the King of the Jews, save thyself.
23.38: And there was also a superscription written over him in Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew letters: This is The King of The Jews.
23.39: And one of those thieves that were hanged, blasphemed him, saying: If thou be Christ, save thyself, and us.
23.40: But the other answering, rebuked him, saying: Neither doest thou fear God, where as thou art in the same damnation?
23.41: And we indeed justly, for we receive worthy of our doings: but this man hath done no evil.
23.42: And he said to Jesus: Lord, remember me when thou shalt come into thy Kingdom.
23.43: And Jesus said to him: Amen I say to thee; this day thou shalt be with me in Paradise.
23.44: And it was almost the sixth hour: and there was made darkness upon the whole earth until the ninth hour.
23.45: And the sun was darkened; and the veil of the temple was rent in the midst.
23.46: And Jesus crying with a loud voice, said: Father, into thy hands I commend my Spirit. And saying this, he gave up the ghost.
23.47: And the Centurion seeing that which was done, glorified God, saying: Indeed this man was just.
23.48: And all the multitude of them that were present together at that sight, and saw the things that were done, returned knocking their breasts.
23.49: And all his acquaintance stood a far off, and the women that had followed him from Galilee, seeing these things.
23.50: And behold a man named Joseph, which was a Senator, a good man and a just.
23.51: He had not consented to their counsel and doings, of Arimathæa a city of Jewry, who also himself expected the Kingdom of God.
23.52: This man came to Pilate, and asked the body of Jesus.
23.53: And taking it down, wrapped it in sindon, and laid him in a monument hewed of stone, wherein never yet any man had been laid.
23.54: And it was the day of Parasceve, and the Sabboth drew near.
23.55: And the women that were come with him from Galilee, following after, saw the monument, and how his body was laid.
23.56: And returning they prepared spices and ointments: and on the Sabboth they rested according to the commandment.

24.1: And in the first of the Sabboth very early they came to the monument, carrying the spices which they had prepared.
24.2: And they found the stone rolled back from the monument.
24.3: And going in, they found not the body of our Lord Jesus.
24.4: And it came to pass, as they were astonished in their mind at this, behold two men stood beside them in glistering apparel.
24.5: And when they feared and cast down their countenance toward the ground, they said unto them: Why seek you the living with the dead?
24.6: he is not here, but is risen. Remember how he spake to you, when he yet was in Galilee,
24.7: saying: That the Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinners and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
24.9: And going back from the monument, they told all these things to those eleven, and to all the rest.
24.10: And it was Mary Magdalene, and Joane, and Mary of James, and the rest that were with them, which said these things to the Apostles.
24.11: And these words seemed before them as dotage, and they did not believe them.
24.12: But Peter rising up ran to the monument; and stooping down he saw the linen cloths lying alone, and went away marveling with himself at that which was done.
24.13: And behold, two of them went the same day into a town which was the space of sixty furlongs from Jerusalem, named Emmaus.
24.14: And they talked betwixt themselves of all those things that had chanced.
24.15: And it came to pass, while they talked and reasoned with themselves, Jesus also himself approaching went with them.
24.16: But their eyes were held that they might not know him.
24.17: And he said to them: What are these communications that you confer one with another walking, and are sad?
24.18: And one whose name was Cleophas, answering, said to him: Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things that have been done in it, these days?
24.19: To whom he said: What things? And they said, concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a man a Prophet, mighty in work and word before God and all the people.
24.20: And how our chief Priests and Princes delivered him into condemnation of death, and crucified him.
24.21: But we hoped that it was he that should redeem Israel: and now besides all this, today is the third day since these things were done.
24.22: But certain women also of ours, made us afraid: who before it was light, were at the monument,
24.23: and not finding his body, came, saying that they saw a vision also of Angels, who say that he is alive.
24.24: And certain men of ours went to the monument: and they found it so as the women said, but him they found not.
24.25: And he said to them: O foolish, and slow of heart to believe in all things which the Prophets have spoken.
24.26: Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and so to enter into his glory?
24.27: And beginning from Moyses and all the Prophets, he did interpret to them in all the scriptures the things that were concerning him.
24.28: And they drew nigh to the town whither they went: and he made semblance to go further.
24.29: And they forced him, saying: Tarry with us, because it is toward night, and the day is now far spent. And he went in with them.
24.30: And it came to pass, whiles he sat at the table with them, he took bread, and blessed and brake, and did reach to them.
24.31: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him: and he vanished out of their sight.
24.32: And they said one to the other: Was not our heart burning in us, whiles he spake in the way, and opened unto us the scriptures?
24.33: And rising up the same hour they went back into Jerusalem: and they found the eleven gathered together, and those that were with them,
24.34: saying: That our Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
24.35: And they told the things that were done in the way: and how they knew him in the breaking of bread.
24.36: And whiles they speak these things, Jesus stood in the midst of them, and he saith to them: Peace be to you; it is I, fear not.
24.37: But they being troubled and frighted, imagined that they saw a Spirit.
24.38: And he said to them: Why are you troubled, and cogitations arise into your hearts?
24.39: See my hands, and feet, that it is I myself: handle, and see: for a Spirit hath not flesh and bones, as you see me to have.
24.40: And when he had said this, he shewed them his hands and feet.
24.41: But they yet not believing and marveling for joy, he said: Have you here any thing to be eaten?
24.42: But they offered him a piece of fish broiled, and a honey comb.
24.43: And when he had eaten before them, taking the remains he gave to them.
24.44: And he said to them: These are the words which I spake to you, when I was yet with you, that all things must needs be fulfilled, which are written in the law of Moyses, and the Prophets, and the Psalms, of me.
24.45: Then he opened their understanding, that they might understand the Scriptures.
24.46: And he said to them: That so it is written, and so it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise again from the dead the third day:
24.47: and penance to be preached in his name and remission of sins unto all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
24.48: And you are witnesses of these things.
24.49: And I send the promise of my Father upon you: but you, tarry in the city, till you be endued with power from high.
24.50: And he brought them forth abroad into Bethania: and lifting up his hands he blessed them.
24.51: And it came to pass whiles he blessed them, he departed from them, and was carried into Heaven.
24.52: And they adoring went back into Jerusalem with great joy:
24.53: and they were always in the temple praising and blessing God. Amen.
